text.NRSV: Then Pashhur struck the prophet Jeremiah, and put him in the stocks that were in the upper Benjamin Gate of the house of the Lord.
text.Rotherham: then Pashhur smote Jeremiah the prophet,—and put him in the stocks that were in the upper gate of Benjamin, which was in the house of Yahweh.
text.KJV: 20:2 Then Pashur smote Ieremiah the Prophet, and put him in the stockes that were in the high gate of Beniamin, which was by the house of the Lord.
text.NABRE: So he struck the prophet and put him in the stocks at the upper Gate of Benjamin in the house of the Lord.

text.NRSV: The next morning when Pashhur released Jeremiah from the stocks, Jeremiah said to him, “The Lord has named you not ‘Pashhur’ but ‘Terror-all-around.’
text.Rotherham: And it came to pass on the morrow, when Pashhur brought forth Jeremiah out of the stocks,—that Jeremiah said unto him—Not Pashhur, hath Yahweh called thy name, But Magor-missaviv[="Terror-round-about"].
text.KJV: 20:3 And it came to passe on the morrow, that Pashur brought foorth Ieremiah out of the stockes. Then sayd Ieremiah vnto him, The Lord hath not called thy name Pashur, but Magor-missabib.
text.NABRE: The next morning, after Pashhur had released Jeremiah from the stocks, the prophet said to him: “Instead of Pashhur, the Lord names you ‘Terror on every side.’

text.NRSV: For thus says the Lord: I am making you a terror to yourself and to all your friends; and they shall fall by the sword of their enemies while you look on. And I will give all Judah into the hand of the king of Babylon; he shall carry them captive to Babylon, and shall kill them with the sword.
text.Rotherham: For, Thus, saith Yahweh—Behold me! making thee a ["magor" i.e. a] terror to thyself and to all who love thee, and they shall fall by the sword of their enemies, thine own eyes also, beholding, And all Judah, will I deliver into the hand of the king of Babylon, and he will carry them captive to Babylon and smite them with the sword.
text.KJV: 20:4 For thus sayth the Lord, Behold, I will make thee a terrour to thy selfe, and to all thy friends, and they shall fall by the sword of their enemies, and thine eyes shall behold it, and I will giue all Iudah into the hand of the king of Babylon, and hee shall cary them captiue into Babylon, and shall slay them with the sword.
text.NABRE: For thus says the Lord: Indeed, I will hand you over to terror, you and all your friends. Your own eyes shall see them fall by the sword of their enemies. All Judah I will hand over to the power of the king of Babylon, who shall take them captive to Babylon or strike them down with the sword.

text.NRSV: I will give all the wealth of this city, all its gains, all its prized belongings, and all the treasures of the kings of Judah into the hand of their enemies, who shall plunder them, and seize them, and carry them to Babylon.
text.Rotherham: And I will deliver up—All the wealth of this city, and All her labour, and All her precious things,—And all the treasures of the kings of Judah, will I deliver up into the hands of their enemies, and they will make of them a prey, and take them, and carry them into Babylon.
text.KJV: 20:5 Moreouer, I will deliuer all the strength of this city, and all the labours thereof, and all the precious things thereof, and all the treasures of the kings of Iudah will I giue into the hand of their enemies which shal spoile them, and take them and cary them to Babylon.
text.NABRE: All the wealth of this city, all its resources and its valuables, all the treasures of the kings of Judah, I will hand over to their enemies, who will plunder it and carry it away to Babylon.

text.NRSV: And you, Pashhur, and all who live in your house, shall go into captivity, and to Babylon you shall go; there you shall die, and there you shall be buried, you and all your friends, to whom you have prophesied falsely.”
text.Rotherham: And, thou, Pashhur and all who are dwelling in thy house, shall go into captivity,—Yea into Babylon, shalt thou enter And there, shalt thou die And there, shalt thou be buried Thou and all who love thee, to whom thou hast prophesied falsely.
text.KJV: 20:6 And thou Pashur, and all that dwell in thine house, shall goe into captiuitie, and thou shalt come to Babylon, and there thou shalt die, and shalt be buried there, thou and all thy friends to whom thou hast prophecied lies.
text.NABRE: You, Pashhur, and all the members of your household shall go into exile. To Babylon you shall go; there you shall die and be buried, you and all your friends, because you have prophesied lies to them.”

text.NRSV: O Lord, you have enticed me, and I was enticed; you have overpowered me, and you have prevailed. I have become a laughingstock all day long; everyone mocks me.
text.Rotherham: Thou didst persuade me, O Yahweh and I was persuaded, Thou didst lay firm hold on me and didst prevail,—I am become a mockery, all the day, Every one, is laughing at me.
text.KJV: 20:7 ¶ O Lord, thou hast deceiued me, and I was deceiued, thou art stronger then I, and hast preuailed: I am in derision daily, euery one mocketh me.
text.NABRE: Jeremiah’s Interior Crisis You seduced me, Lord, and I let myself be seduced; you were too strong for me, and you prevailed. All day long I am an object of laughter; everyone mocks me.

text.NRSV: For whenever I speak, I must cry out, I must shout, “Violence and destruction!” For the word of the Lord has become for me a reproach and derision all day long.
text.Rotherham: For, as often as I speak, I make outcry, Violence and wasting, I proclaim,—Yea the word of Yahweh hath become to me a reproach and derision all the day
text.KJV: 20:8 For since I spake I cryed out, I cried violence and spoyle; because the word of the Lord was made a reproch vnto me, and a derision daily?
text.NABRE: Whenever I speak, I must cry out, violence and outrage I proclaim; The word of the Lord has brought me reproach and derision all day long.

text.NRSV: If I say, “I will not mention him, or speak any more in his name,” then within me there is something like a burning fire shut up in my bones; I am weary with holding it in, and I cannot.
text.Rotherham: Therefore I say—I will not mention him Neither will I speak any more in his name, But then it becometh in my heart as a fire that burneth, Shut up in my bones,—And I am weary of restraint and cannot refrain.
text.KJV: 20:9 Then I said; I will not make mention of him, nor speake any more in his name. But his word was in mine heart, as a burning fire shut vp in my bones, and I was weary with forbearing, and I could not stay.
text.NABRE: I say I will not mention him, I will no longer speak in his name. But then it is as if fire is burning in my heart, imprisoned in my bones; I grow weary holding back, I cannot!

text.NRSV: For I hear many whispering: “Terror is all around! Denounce him! Let us denounce him!” All my close friends are watching for me to stumble. “Perhaps he can be enticed, and we can prevail against him, and take our revenge on him.”
text.Rotherham: Because I have heard the whispering of many—"A terror round about!" Tell ye [say they], that we may tell of him, All the men I am wont to salute do watch for my halting,—Peradventure he will be persuaded, and we shall prevail over him, and take our vengeance upon him.
text.KJV: 20:10 ¶ For I heard the defaming of many, feare on euery side. Report, say they, and wee will report it: all my familiars watched for my halting, saying; Peraduenture he will be enticed: and we shall preuaile against him, and we shall take our reuenge on him.
text.NABRE: Yes, I hear the whisperings of many: “Terror on every side! Denounce! let us denounce him!” All those who were my friends are on the watch for any misstep of mine. “Perhaps he can be tricked; then we will prevail, and take our revenge on him.”

text.NRSV: But the Lord is with me like a dread warrior; therefore my persecutors will stumble, and they will not prevail. They will be greatly shamed, for they will not succeed. Their eternal dishonor will never be forgotten.
text.Rotherham: But, Yahweh, is with me, as a mighty one striking terror, For this cause, shall my persecutors stumble and not prevail,—They have turned very pale, For they have not prospered, Confusion age-abiding, it shall not be forgotten!
text.KJV: 20:11 But the Lord is with me as a mighty terrible one: therefore my persecutours shall stumble, and they shall not preuaile, they shall be greatly ashamed, for they shall not prosper, their euerlasting confusion shall neuer be forgotten.
text.NABRE: But the Lord is with me, like a mighty champion: my persecutors will stumble, they will not prevail. In their failure they will be put to utter shame, to lasting, unforgettable confusion.

text.NRSV: O Lord of hosts, you who test the righteous, who see the heart and the mind, let me see your retribution upon them, for to you I have committed my cause.
text.Rotherham: But O Yahweh of hosts—Testing the righteous Beholding the affections and the heart,—Let me see thine avenging upon them, For unto thee, have I laid bare my cause.
text.KJV: 20:12 But O Lord of hostes, that tryest the righteous, and seest the reines and the heart, let me see thy vengeance on them: for vnto thee haue I opened my cause.
text.NABRE: Lord of hosts, you test the just, you see mind and heart, Let me see the vengeance you take on them, for to you I have entrusted my cause.

text.NRSV: Sing to the Lord; praise the Lord! For he has delivered the life of the needy from the hands of evildoers.
text.Rotherham: Sing ye to Yahweh! Praise ye Yahweh! For he hath delivered the soul of the needy, out of the hand of evildoers.
text.KJV: 20:13 Sing vnto the Lord, praise yee the Lord: for hee hath deliuered the soule of the poore from the hand of euill doers.
text.NABRE: Sing to the Lord, praise the Lord, For he has rescued the life of the poor from the power of the evildoers!

text.NRSV: Cursed be the day on which I was born! The day when my mother bore me, let it not be blessed!
text.Rotherham: Accursed, be the day on which I was born,—The day when my mother bare me, let it not be blessed!
text.KJV: 20:14 ¶ Cursed be the day wherein I was borne: let not the day wherein my mother bare mee, be blessed.
text.NABRE: Cursed be the day on which I was born! May the day my mother gave me birth never be blessed!

text.NRSV: Cursed be the man who brought the news to my father, saying, “A child is born to you, a son,” making him very glad.
text.Rotherham: Accursed, be the man who carried tidings to my father saying, There is born to thee a man-child!
text.KJV: 20:15 Cursed be the man who brought tidings to my father, saying: A man child is borne vnto thee, making him very glad.
text.NABRE: Cursed be the one who brought the news to my father, “A child, a son, has been born to you!” filling him with great joy.

text.NRSV: Let that man be like the cities that the Lord overthrew without pity; let him hear a cry in the morning and an alarm at noon,
text.Rotherham: Making him very glad: Yea let that man be—as the cities which Yahweh overthrew and repented not,—And let him hear An outcry in the morning, and A war-shout at broad noon!
text.KJV: 20:16 And let that man be as the cities which the Lord ouerthrew and repented not: and let him heare the cry in the morning, and the shouting at noonetide,
text.NABRE: Let that man be like the cities which the Lord relentlessly overthrew; Let him hear war cries in the morning, battle alarms at noonday,

text.NRSV: because he did not kill me in the womb; so my mother would have been my grave, and her womb forever great.
text.Rotherham: Because I was not slain from the womb,—Nor did my mother become my grave, Nor was her womb great for ever!
text.KJV: 20:17 Because he slew me not from the wombe: or that my mother might haue beene my graue, and her wombe to be alwaies great with me.
text.NABRE: because he did not kill me in the womb! Then my mother would have been my grave, her womb confining me forever.

text.NRSV: Why did I come forth from the womb to see toil and sorrow, and spend my days in shame?
text.Rotherham: Wherefore was it—That from the womb, I came forth, to see labour and pain; and That in shame should my days be consumed!
text.KJV: 20:18 Wherefore came I forth out of the wombe to see labour and sorrow, that my daies should be consumed with shame?
text.NABRE: Why did I come forth from the womb, to see sorrow and pain, to end my days in shame?

text.NRSV: This is the word that came to Jeremiah from the Lord, when King Zedekiah sent to him Pashhur son of Malchiah and the priest Zephaniah son of Maaseiah, saying,
text.Rotherham: The word which came unto Jeremiah from Yahweh,—when King Zedekiah sent unto him Pashhur, son of Malchiah, and Zephaniah, son of Maaseiah, the priest, saying:
text.KJV: 21:1 The word which came vnto Ieremiah from the Lord, when king Zedekiah sent vnto him Pashur the sonne of Melchiah, and Zephaniah the sonne of Maaseiah the priest, saying;
text.NABRE: III. Oracles in the Last Years of Jerusalem Chapter 21 Fate of Zedekiah and Jerusalem. The word which came to Jeremiah from the Lord when King Zedekiah sent Pashhur, son of Malchiah, and the priest Zephaniah, son of Maaseiah, to him with this request:

text.NRSV: “Please inquire of the Lord on our behalf, for King Nebuchadrezzar of Babylon is making war against us; perhaps the Lord will perform for us one of his wonders as of old, and will make him withdraw from us.”
text.Rotherham: Enquire for us I pray thee of Yahweh, in that, Nebuchadrezzar king of Babylon, maketh war against us,—Peradventure Yahweh will deal with us according to all his wonders, so that he go up from us.
text.KJV: 21:2 Enquire, I pray thee, of the Lord for vs (for Nebuchad-rezzar king of Babylon maketh warre against vs) if so be that the Lord will deale with vs, according to all his wondrous workes, that he may goe vp from vs.
text.NABRE: Inquire for us of the Lord, because Nebuchadnezzar, king of Babylon, is attacking us. Perhaps the Lord will act for us in accord with his wonderful works by making him withdraw from us.

text.NRSV: Then Jeremiah said to them: Thus you shall say to Zedekiah:
text.Rotherham: Then said Jeremiah unto them,—Thus, shall ye say unto Zedekiah:
text.KJV: 21:3 ¶ Then saide Ieremiah vnto them. Thus shall yee say to Zedekiah,
text.NABRE: But Jeremiah answered them: This is what you shall report to Zedekiah:

text.NRSV: Thus says the Lord, the God of Israel: I am about to turn back the weapons of war that are in your hands and with which you are fighting against the king of Babylon and against the Chaldeans who are besieging you outside the walls; and I will bring them together into the center of this city.
text.Rotherham: Thus, saith Yahweh, God of Israel—Behold me! turning back the weapons of war that are in your hand, wherewith, ye, are fighting the king of Babylon and the Chaldeans, who are besieging you outside the wall,—and I will gather them into the midst of this city.
text.KJV: 21:4 Thus saith the Lord God of Israel; Behold, I will turne backe the weapons of warre that are in your hands, wherewith yee fight against the king of Babylon, and against the Caldeans, which besiege you without the walles, and I will assemble them into the middest of this citie.
text.NABRE: Thus says the Lord, the God of Israel: I will turn against you the weapons with which you are fighting the king of Babylon and the Chaldeans who besiege you outside the walls. These weapons I will pile up in the midst of this city,

text.NRSV: I myself will fight against you with outstretched hand and mighty arm, in anger, in fury, and in great wrath.
text.Rotherham: And I myself, will fight against you, with a hand outstretched, and with an arm of strength,—and with anger and with wrath and with great indignation;
text.KJV: 21:5 And I myselfe will fight against you with an out stretched hand, and with a strong arme, euen in anger, and in furie, and in great wrath.
text.NABRE: and I myself will fight against you with outstretched hand and mighty arm, in anger, wrath, and great rage!

text.NRSV: And I will strike down the inhabitants of this city, both human beings and animals; they shall die of a great pestilence.
text.Rotherham: and I will smite the inhabitants of this city, both man and beast,—of a great pestilence, shall they die.
text.KJV: 21:6 And I will smite the inhabitants of this citie both man and beast: they shall die of a great pestilence.
text.NABRE: I will strike down the inhabitants of this city, human being and beast; they shall die in a great pestilence.

text.NRSV: Afterward, says the Lord, I will give King Zedekiah of Judah, and his servants, and the people in this city—those who survive the pestilence, sword, and famine—into the hands of King Nebuchadrezzar of Babylon, into the hands of their enemies, into the hands of those who seek their lives. He shall strike them down with the edge of the sword; he shall not spare them, or have pity or compassion.
text.Rotherham: And after that, Declareth Yahweh—Will I deliver Zedekiah king of Judah and his servants, and the people—even such as are left in this city from the pestilence from the sword and from the famine,—into the hand of Nebuchadrezzar king of Babylon, even into the hand of their enemies, and into the hand of them who are seeking their life,—and he will smite them with the edge of the sword, he will not have pity on them nor will he spare nor will he have compassion.
text.KJV: 21:7 And afterward, saith the Lord, I will deliuer Zedekiah king of Iudah, and his seruants, and the people, and such as are left in this citie from the pestilence, from the sword, and from the famine, into the hand of Nebuchadrezzar king of Babylon, and into the hand of their enemies, and into the hand of those that seeke their life, and and he shall smite them with the edge of the sword: hee shall not spare them, neither haue pitie, nor haue mercy.
text.NABRE: After that—oracle of the Lord —I will hand over Zedekiah, king of Judah, and his ministers and the people in this city who survive pestilence, sword, and famine, to Nebuchadnezzar, king of Babylon, to their enemies and those who seek their lives. He shall strike them down with the edge of the sword, without quarter, without mercy or compassion.

text.NRSV: And to this people you shall say: Thus says the Lord: See, I am setting before you the way of life and the way of death.
text.Rotherham: And unto this people, shalt thou say, Thus, saith Yahweh,—Behold me! setting before you the way, of life, and the way of death:
text.KJV: 21:8 ¶ And vnto this people thou shalt say; Thus saith the Lord; Behold, I set before you the way of life, and the way of death.
text.NABRE: And to this people you shall say: Thus says the Lord: See, I am giving you a choice between the way to life and the way to death.

text.NRSV: Those who stay in this city shall die by the sword, by famine, and by pestilence; but those who go out and surrender to the Chaldeans who are besieging you shall live and shall have their lives as a prize of war.
text.Rotherham: He that remaineth in this city, shall die—by the sword or by the famine or by the pestilence,—Whereas, he that goeth forth and falleth unto the Chaldeans, who are besieging you,—then shall he live, and, his life, shall become to him, a spoil;
text.KJV: 21:9 He that abideth in this citie, shall die by the sword, and by the famine, and by the pestilence: but he that goeth out, and falleth to the Caldeans, that besiege you, he shall liue, and his life shall be vnto him, for a pray.
text.NABRE: Whoever remains in this city shall die by the sword or famine or pestilence. But whoever leaves and surrenders to the Chaldeans who are besieging you shall live and escape with his life.

text.NRSV: For I have set my face against this city for evil and not for good, says the Lord: it shall be given into the hands of the king of Babylon, and he shall burn it with fire.
text.Rotherham: For I have set my face against this city, for calamity and not for blessing, Declareth Yahweh,—Into the hand of the king of Babylon, shall it be given up, and he will burn it with fire.
text.KJV: 21:10 For I haue set my face against this citie for euill and not for good, saith the Lord; it shall be giuen into the hand of the king of Babylon, and he shall burne it with fire.
text.NABRE: I have set my face against this city, for evil and not for good—oracle of the Lord. It shall be given into the power of the king of Babylon who shall set it on fire.

text.NRSV: To the royal house of Judah say: Hear the word of the Lord,
text.Rotherham: Now, as to the house of the king of Judah,—hear ye the word of Yahweh:—
text.KJV: 21:11 ¶ And touching the house of the king of Iudah, say; Heare yee the word of the Lord.
text.NABRE: Oracles Regarding the Kings To the royal house of Judah: Hear the word of the Lord,

text.NRSV: O house of David! Thus says the Lord: Execute justice in the morning, and deliver from the hand of the oppressor anyone who has been robbed, or else my wrath will go forth like fire, and burn with no one to quench it, because of your evil doings.
text.Rotherham: O house of David! Thus, saith Yahweh, Administer justice betimes, And deliver the robbed out of the hand of the oppressor,—Lest mine indignation come forth like fire And burn and there be none to quench it, Because of the wickedness of your doings:
text.KJV: 21:12 Oh house of Dauid, thus saith the Lord, Execute iudgement in the morning, and deliuer him that is spoiled, out of the hand of the oppressour, lest my furie goe out like fire, and burne, that none can quench it, because of the euill of your doings.
text.NABRE: house of David! Thus says the Lord: Each morning dispense justice, rescue the oppressed from the hand of the oppressor, Or my fury will break out like fire and burn with no one to quench it because of your evil deeds.

text.NRSV: See, I am against you, O inhabitant of the valley, O rock of the plain, says the Lord; you who say, “Who can come down against us, or who can enter our abodes?”
text.Rotherham: Behold me! against thee, O thou dweller in the vale on the level rock, Declareth Yahweh,—Ye who are saying, Who shall come down upon us? Who shall enter our habitations?
text.KJV: 21:13 Behold, I am against thee, O inhabitant of the valley, and rocke of the plaine, saith the Lord, which say, Who shall come downe against vs? or who shall enter into our habitations?
text.NABRE: Beware! I am against you, Ruler of the Valley, Rock of the Plain —oracle of the Lord. You say, “Who will attack us, who can storm our defenses?”

text.NRSV: I will punish you according to the fruit of your doings, says the Lord; I will kindle a fire in its forest, and it shall devour all that is around it.
text.Rotherham: Yet will I bring punishment upon you according to the fruit of your doings, Declareth Yahweh,—and will kindle a fire in her forest, and it shall devour all things round about her.
text.KJV: 21:14 But I will punish you according to the fruit of your doings, saith the Lord: and I will kindle a fire in the forrest thereof, and it shall deuoure all things round about it.
text.NABRE: I will punish you—oracle of the Lord — as your deeds deserve! I will kindle a fire in its forest that shall devour all its surroundings.

text.NRSV: Thus says the Lord: Go down to the palace of the king of Judah, and speak there this word,
text.Rotherham: Thus, said Yahweh, Go thou down to the house of king of Judah, and speak thou there this word,
text.KJV: 22:1 Thus saith the Lord, Goe downe to the house of the king of Iudah, and speake there this word,
text.NABRE: Chapter 22 Thus says the Lord: Go down to the palace of the king of Judah and there deliver this word:

text.NRSV: and say: Hear the word of the Lord, O King of Judah sitting on the throne of David—you, and your servants, and your people who enter these gates.
text.Rotherham: and say—Hear thou the word of Yahweh O king of Judah who sittest upon the throne of David,—thou, and thy servants and thy people who enter in at these gates,
text.KJV: 22:2 And say, Heare the word of the Lord, O king of Iudah, that sittest vpon the throne of Dauid, thou, and thy seruants, and thy people that enter in by these gates.
text.NABRE: You shall say: Listen to the word of the Lord, king of Judah, who sit on the throne of David, you, your ministers, and your people who enter by these gates!

text.NRSV: Thus says the Lord: Act with justice and righteousness, and deliver from the hand of the oppressor anyone who has been robbed. And do no wrong or violence to the alien, the orphan, and the widow, or shed innocent blood in this place.
text.Rotherham: Thus, saith Yahweh,—Execute ye justice, and righteousness, And deliver the robbed out of the hand of the oppressor,—But the sojourner the fatherless, and the widow, do not oppress neither commit violence, And the blood of the innocent, do not ye shed, in this place.
text.KJV: 22:3 Thus saith the Lord, Execute ye iudgement and righteousnesse, and deliuer the spoiler out of the hand of the oppressour: and doe no wrong, doe no violence to the stranger, the fatherlesse, nor the widow, neither shed innocent blood in this place.
text.NABRE: Thus says the Lord: Do what is right and just. Rescue the victims from the hand of their oppressors. Do not wrong or oppress the resident alien, the orphan, or the widow, and do not shed innocent blood in this place.

text.NRSV: For if you will indeed obey this word, then through the gates of this house kings who sit on the throne of David shall enter, riding in chariots and on horses, they, and their servants, and their people.
text.Rotherham: For if ye, indeed do, this thing, then shall there enter into the gates of this house—kings, sitting for David upon his throne riding in chariots and on horses, he and his servants, and his people.
text.KJV: 22:4 For if ye doe this thing indeede, then shall there enter in by the gates of this house, Kings sitting vpon the throne of Dauid, riding in charets and on horses, he, and his seruants, and his people.
text.NABRE: If you carry out these commands, kings who succeed to the throne of David will continue to enter the gates of this house, riding in chariots or mounted on horses, with their ministers, and their people.

text.NRSV: But if you will not heed these words, I swear by myself, says the Lord, that this house shall become a desolation.
text.Rotherham: But if ye will not hear these words, By myself have I sworn Declareth Yahweh—That, a ruin, shall this house become.
text.KJV: 22:5 But if yee will not heare these words, I sweare by my selfe, saith the Lord, that this house shall become a desolation.
text.NABRE: But if you do not obey these commands, I swear by myself—oracle of the Lord: this house shall become rubble.

text.NRSV: For thus says the Lord concerning the royal house of Judah: You are like Gilead to me, like the summit of Lebanon; but I swear that I will make you a desert, an uninhabited city.
text.Rotherham: For Thus, saith Yahweh Concerning the house of the king of Judah,—Though thou wast Gilead, to me, The summit of Lebanon, Yet surely I will make thee, A wilderness, Cities, not habitable;
text.KJV: 22:6 For thus saith the Lord vnto the kings house of Iudah, Thou art Gilead vnto me, and the head of Lebanon: yet surely I will make thee a wildernesse, and cities which are not inhabited.
text.NABRE: For thus says the Lord concerning the house of the king of Judah: Though you be to me like Gilead, like the peak of Lebanon, I swear I shall turn you into a waste, with cities uninhabited.

text.NRSV: I will prepare destroyers against you, all with their weapons; they shall cut down your choicest cedars and cast them into the fire.
text.Rotherham: And I will hallow against thee Destroyers Every man with his weapons,—And they shall cut down of the choicest of thy cedars, and cast upon the fire.
text.KJV: 22:7 And I will prepare destroyers against thee, euery one with his weapons, and they shall cut downe thy choise cedars, and cast them into the fire.
text.NABRE: Against you I will send destroyers, each with their tools: They shall cut down your choice cedars, and cast them into the fire.

text.NRSV: And many nations will pass by this city, and all of them will say one to another, “Why has the Lord dealt in this way with that great city?”
text.Rotherham: Then shall many nations pass by this city,—and shall say, every man to his neighbour, For what cause, hath Yahweh done thus, unto this great city?
text.KJV: 22:8 And many nations shall passe by this citie, and they shall say euery man to his neighbour, Wherefore hath the Lord done thus vnto this great citie?
text.NABRE: Many nations will pass by this city and ask one another: “Why has the Lord done this to so great a city?”

text.NRSV: And they will answer, “Because they abandoned the covenant of the Lord their God, and worshiped other gods and served them.”
text.Rotherham: And they shall say, Because they forsook the covenant of Yahweh, their God,—And bowed down to other gods And served them.
text.KJV: 22:9 Then they shall answere, Because they haue forsaken the couenant of the Lord their God, and worshipped other gods, and serued them.
text.NABRE: And they will be told: “Because they have deserted their covenant with the Lord, their God, by worshiping and serving other gods.”

text.NRSV: Do not weep for him who is dead, nor bemoan him; weep rather for him who goes away, for he shall return no more to see his native land.
text.Rotherham: Do not ye lament for him that is dead Neither bemoan ye, him, But weep ye—weep on—for him that is going away, For he shall not return any more Nor see the land of his birth.
text.KJV: 22:10 ¶ Weepe ye not for the dead, neither bemoane him, but weepe sore for him that goeth away: for he shall returne no more, nor see his natiue countrey.
text.NABRE: Jehoahaz Do not weep for him who is dead, nor mourn for him! Weep rather for him who is going away; never again to see the land of his birth.

text.NRSV: For thus says the Lord concerning Shallum son of King Josiah of Judah, who succeeded his father Josiah, and who went forth from this place: He shall return here no more,
text.Rotherham: For Thus, saith Yahweh—Touching Shallum, son of Josiah king of Judah, That reigneth instead of Josiah his father, Who hath gone forth out of this place, He shall not return thither any more;
text.KJV: 22:11 For thus saith the Lord touching Shallum, the sonne of Iosiah king of Iudah which reigned in stead of Iosiah his father, which went forth out of this place, He shall not returne thither any more.
text.NABRE: Thus says the Lord concerning Shallum, son of Josiah, king of Judah, his father’s successor, who left this place: He shall never return,

text.NRSV: but in the place where they have carried him captive, there he shall die, and he shall never see this land again.
text.Rotherham: For in the place whither they have taken him captive, There, shall he die,—And this land, shall he see no more.
text.KJV: 22:12 But he shall die in the place whither they haue led him captiue, and shal see this land no more.
text.NABRE: but in the place where they exiled him, there he shall die; he shall never see this land again.

text.NRSV: Woe to him who builds his house by unrighteousness, and his upper rooms by injustice; who makes his neighbors work for nothing, and does not give them their wages;
text.Rotherham: Alas! for him who buildeth his house without righteousness, And his roof-chambers without justice,—Of his neighbour, taketh service for nought, And recompense for his work, giveth him not.
text.KJV: 22:13 ¶ Woe vnto him that buildeth his house by vnrighteousnesse, and his chambers by wrong: that vseth his neighbours seruice without wages, and giueth him not for his worke:
text.NABRE: Jehoiakim Woe to him who builds his house on wrongdoing, his roof-chambers on injustice; Who works his neighbors without pay, and gives them no wages.

text.NRSV: who says, “I will build myself a spacious house with large upper rooms,” and who cuts out windows for it, paneling it with cedar, and painting it with vermilion.
text.Rotherham: Who saith—I will build me a roomy house, with spacious roof-chambers,—So he cutteth him open its windows, And it is covered in with cedar, And he painteth it with vermilion.
text.KJV: 22:14 That saith, I will build mee a wide house and large chambers, and cutteth him out windowes, and it is sieled with cedar, and painted with vermilion.
text.NABRE: Who says, “I will build myself a spacious house, with airy rooms,” Who cuts out windows for it, panels it with cedar, and paints it with vermilion.

text.NRSV: Are you a king because you compete in cedar? Did not your father eat and drink and do justice and righteousness? Then it was well with him.
text.Rotherham: Shalt thou reign, because, thou, art eager to excel in cedar? Thy father, did he not eat and drink, and do justice and righteousness, And, then, it was well with him?
text.KJV: 22:15 Shalt thou reigne because thou closest thy selfe in cedar? did not thy father eate and drinke, and doe iudgment and iustice, and then it was wel with him?
text.NABRE: Must you prove your rank among kings by competing with them in cedar? Did not your father eat and drink, And act justly and righteously? Then he prospered.

text.NRSV: He judged the cause of the poor and needy; then it was well. Is not this what it means to know me? says the Lord.
text.Rotherham: [Did he not] plead the cause of the oppressed and the needy, And, then, it was well? Was not, that, to know, me? Demandeth Yahweh.
text.KJV: 22:16 He iudged the cause of the poore and needy, then it was well with him: was not this to know me, saith the Lord ?
text.NABRE: Because he dispensed justice to the weak and the poor, he prospered. Is this not to know me?— oracle of the Lord.

text.NRSV: But your eyes and heart are only on your dishonest gain, for shedding innocent blood, and for practicing oppression and violence.
text.Rotherham: Verily thou hast neither eyes nor heart, save for thy plundering and for thy shedding of innocent blood, and for oppression and for crushing to do them!
text.KJV: 22:17 But thine eyes and thine heart are not but for thy couetousnesse, and for to shed innocent blood, and for oppression, and for violence to doe it.
text.NABRE: But your eyes and heart are set on nothing except your own gain, On shedding innocent blood and practicing oppression and extortion.

text.NRSV: Therefore thus says the Lord concerning King Jehoiakim son of Josiah of Judah: They shall not lament for him, saying, “Alas, my brother!” or “Alas, sister!” They shall not lament for him, saying, “Alas, lord!” or “Alas, his majesty!”
text.Rotherham: Therefore—Thus, saith Yahweh, Touching Jehoiakim son of Josiah King of Judah, They shall not cry in lament for him Alas my brother! or Alas sister! They shall not cry in lament for him Alas lord! or Alas! his renown!
text.KJV: 22:18 Therefore thus saith the Lord concerning Iehoiakim the sonne of Iosiah king of Iudah, They shall not lament for him, saying, Ah my brother, or ah sister: they shall not lament for him, saying, Ah Lord, or ah his glory.
text.NABRE: Therefore, thus says the Lord concerning Jehoiakim, son of Josiah, king of Judah: They shall not lament him, “Alas! my brother”; “Alas! sister.” They shall not lament him, “Alas, Lord! alas, Majesty!”

text.NRSV: With the burial of a donkey he shall be buried— dragged off and thrown out beyond the gates of Jerusalem.
text.Rotherham: With the burial of an ass, shall he be buried, Dragged along and cast forth, beyond the gates of Jerusalem.
text.KJV: 22:19 He shall be buried with the buriall of an asse, drawen and cast forth beyond the gates of Ierusalem.
text.NABRE: The burial of a donkey he shall be given, dragged forth and cast out beyond the gates of Jerusalem.

text.NRSV: Go up to Lebanon, and cry out, and lift up your voice in Bashan; cry out from Abarim, for all your lovers are crushed.
text.Rotherham: Ascend the Lebanon and make outcry, And in Bashan, put forth thy voice,—And make outcry from Abarim, For all thy lovers are torn in pieces.
text.KJV: 22:20 ¶ Goe vp to Lebanon, and crie, and lift vp thy voice in Bashan, and crie from the passages: for all thy louers are destroyed.
text.NABRE: Jeconiah Climb Lebanon and cry out, in Bashan lift up your voice; Cry out from Abarim, for all your lovers are crushed.

text.NRSV: I spoke to you in your prosperity, but you said, “I will not listen.” This has been your way from your youth, for you have not obeyed my voice.
text.Rotherham: I spake unto thee, in thy carelessness,—Thou saidst I will not hearken! This hath been thy way from thy youth, That thou hast not hearkened to my voice.
text.KJV: 22:21 I spake vnto thee in thy prosperitie, but thou saidest, I will not heare: this hath bin thy maner from thy youth, that thou obeyedst not my voice.
text.NABRE: I spoke to you when you were secure, but you answered, “I will not listen.” This has been your way from your youth, not to listen to my voice.

text.NRSV: The wind shall shepherd all your shepherds, and your lovers shall go into captivity; then you will surely be ashamed and dismayed because of all your wickedness.
text.Rotherham: All thy shepherds, the wind shall feed, And, thy lovers into captivity, shall depart,—Surely, then shalt thou turn pale and be confounded, by reason of all thy wickedness.
text.KJV: 22:22 The winde shall eate vp all thy pastors, and thy louers shall goe into captiuitie, surely then shalt thou be ashamed and confounded for all thy wickednesse.
text.NABRE: The wind shall shepherd all your shepherds, your lovers shall go into exile. Surely then you shall be ashamed and confounded because of all your wickedness.

text.NRSV: O inhabitant of Lebanon, nested among the cedars, how you will groan when pangs come upon you, pain as of a woman in labor!
text.Rotherham: O inhabitress of Lebanon that makest thy nest in the cedars,—How hast thou bemoaned thyself Now that pangs have overtaken thee, Anguish, as of her that giveth birth.
text.KJV: 22:23 O inhabitant of Lebanon, that makest thy nest in the Cedars, how gracious shalt thou bee when pangs come vpon thee, the paine as of a woman in trauell?
text.NABRE: You who dwell on Lebanon, who nest in the cedars, How you shall groan when pains come upon you, like the pangs of a woman in childbirth!

text.NRSV: As I live, says the Lord, even if King Coniah son of Jehoiakim of Judah were the signet ring on my right hand, even from there I would tear you off
text.Rotherham: As I live, Declareth Yahweh,—Even though Coniah son of Jehoiakim king of Judah were the signet-ring upon my right hand, yet, from thence, would I pull thee off;
text.KJV: 22:24 As I liue, saith the Lord, though Coniah the sonne of Iehoiakim king of Iudah were the signet vpon my right hand, yet would I plucke thee thence.
text.NABRE: As I live—oracle of the Lord —even if you, Coniah, son of Jehoiakim, king of Judah, were a signet ring on my right hand, I would snatch you off.

text.NRSV: and give you into the hands of those who seek your life, into the hands of those of whom you are afraid, even into the hands of King Nebuchadrezzar of Babylon and into the hands of the Chaldeans.
text.Rotherham: and I would give thee into the hand of them who seek thy life and into the hand of them from the face of whom, thou dost shrink in fear,—even into the hand of Nebuchadrezzar king of Babylon and into the hand of the Chaldeans;
text.KJV: 22:25 And I will giue thee into the hand of them that seeke thy life, and into the hand of them whose face thou fearest, euen into the hand of Nebuchad-rezzar king of Babylon, and into the hand of the Caldeans.
text.NABRE: I will hand you over to those who seek your life, to those you dread: Nebuchadnezzar, king of Babylon, and the Chaldeans.

text.NRSV: I will hurl you and the mother who bore you into another country, where you were not born, and there you shall die.
text.Rotherham: and I will hurl thee out and thy mother who bare thee, upon another land where ye were not born,—and, there, shall ye die.
text.KJV: 22:26 And I will cast thee out, and thy mother that bare thee, into another countrey where ye were not borne, and there shall ye die.
text.NABRE: I will cast you out, you and the mother who bore you, into a land different from the land of your birth; and there you will die;

text.NRSV: But to the land to which they long to return, there they shall not return.
text.Rotherham: But unto the land whither they shall be lifting up their souls to return, thither, shall they not return.
text.KJV: 22:27 But to the land whereunto they desire to returne, thither shall they not returne.
text.NABRE: Neither shall return to the land for which they yearn.

text.NRSV: Is this man Coniah a despised, broken pot, a vessel no one wants? Why are he and his offspring hurled out and cast into a land that they do not know?
text.Rotherham: An earthen vessel to be despised thrown about, is this man Coniah? Or an instrument, in which is no pleasure? Wherefore are they to be cast out, he and his seed, and to be thrown forth upon a land which they have not known?
text.KJV: 22:28 Is this man Coniah a despised broken idole? is hee a vessell wherein is no pleasure? wherefore are they cast out, he and his seed, and are cast into a land which they know not?
text.NABRE: Is this man Coniah a thing despised, to be broken, a vessel that no one wants? Why are he and his offspring cast out? why thrown into a land they do not know?

text.NRSV: O land, land, land, hear the word of the Lord!
text.Rotherham: O land, land, land! Hear thou the word of Yahweh!
text.KJV: 22:29 O earth, earth, earth, heare the word of the Lord:
text.NABRE: O land, land, land, hear the word of the Lord —

text.NRSV: Thus says the Lord: Record this man as childless, a man who shall not succeed in his days; for none of his offspring shall succeed in sitting on the throne of David, and ruling again in Judah.
text.Rotherham: Thus, saith Yahweh,—Register ye this man, childless, A man who shall not prosper in his days,—For there shall prosper of his seed No man sitting upon the throne of David, Or ruling any more over Judah.
text.KJV: 22:30 Thus saith the Lord, Write ye this man childlesse, a man that shall not prosper in his dayes: for no man of his seed shall prosper, sitting vpon the throne of Dauid, and ruling any more in Iudah.
text.NABRE: Thus says the Lord: Write this man down as childless, a man who will never prosper in his life! Nor shall any of his descendants prosper, to sit upon the throne of David, to rule again over Judah.

text.NRSV: Woe to the shepherds who destroy and scatter the sheep of my pasture! says the Lord.
text.Rotherham: Alas for the shepherds who are destroying and scattering the sheep of my pasture Declareth Yahweh.
text.KJV: 23:1 Woe bee vnto the pastors that destroy and scatter the sheepe of my pasture, saith the Lord.
text.NABRE: Chapter 23 A Just Shepherd. Woe to the shepherds who destroy and scatter the flock of my pasture—oracle of the Lord.

text.NRSV: Therefore thus says the Lord, the God of Israel, concerning the shepherds who shepherd my people: It is you who have scattered my flock, and have driven them away, and you have not attended to them. So I will attend to you for your evil doings, says the Lord.
text.Rotherham: Therefore, Thus, saith Yahweh God of Israel, Concerning the shepherds who are tending my people,—Ye have scattered my flock and have driven them away, and have not visited them,—Behold me! visiting upon you the wickedness of your doings Declareth Yahweh.
text.KJV: 23:2 Therefore thus saith the Lord God of Israel against the pastors that feed my people; Yee haue scattered my flocke and driuen them away, and haue not visited them; behold I will visite vpon you the euill of your doings, saith the Lord.
text.NABRE: Therefore, thus says the Lord, the God of Israel, against the shepherds who shepherd my people: You have scattered my sheep and driven them away. You have not cared for them, but I will take care to punish your evil deeds.

text.NRSV: Then I myself will gather the remnant of my flock out of all the lands where I have driven them, and I will bring them back to their fold, and they shall be fruitful and multiply.
text.Rotherham: I myself, therefore will gather the remnant of my flock, out of all the lands whither I have driven them,—And will bring them back unto their own fold, And they shall be fruitful and multiply;
text.KJV: 23:3 And I wil gather the remnant of my flocke, out of all countreis whither I haue driuen them, and will bring them againe to their foldes, and they shalbe fruitfull and increase.
text.NABRE: I myself will gather the remnant of my flock from all the lands to which I have banished them and bring them back to their folds; there they shall be fruitful and multiply.

text.NRSV: I will raise up shepherds over them who will shepherd them, and they shall not fear any longer, or be dismayed, nor shall any be missing, says the Lord.
text.Rotherham: And I will raise up over them shepherds who will tend them,—So shall they not be afraid any more, nor be dismayed nor be missing, Declareth Yahweh.
text.KJV: 23:4 And I will set vp shepheards ouer them which shall feed them, and they shal feare no more nor be dismaied, neither shall they bee lacking, saith the Lord.
text.NABRE: I will raise up shepherds for them who will shepherd them so that they need no longer fear or be terrified; none shall be missing—oracle of the Lord.

text.NRSV: The days are surely coming, says the Lord, when I will raise up for David a righteous Branch, and he shall reign as king and deal wisely, and shall execute justice and righteousness in the land.
text.Rotherham: Lo! days are coming, Declareth Yahweh, when I will raise up to David A righteous Bud, And he shall reign as king, and prosper, And shall execute justice and righteousness in the land.
text.KJV: 23:5 ¶ Behold, the daies come, saith the Lord, that I wil raise vnto Dauid a righteous branch, and a King shall reigne and prosper, and shall execute iudgement and iustice in the earth.
text.NABRE: See, days are coming—oracle of the Lord — when I will raise up a righteous branch for David; As king he shall reign and govern wisely, he shall do what is just and right in the land.

text.NRSV: In his days Judah will be saved and Israel will live in safety. And this is the name by which he will be called: “The Lord is our righteousness.”
text.Rotherham: In his days, Shall Judah be saved, And Israel abide securely,—And, this, is his name whereby he shall be called Yahweh, our Righteousness.
text.KJV: 23:6 In his dayes Iudah shalbe saued, and Israel shall dwell safely, and this is his Name whereby hee shall be called, The Lord Our Righteousnes.
text.NABRE: In his days Judah shall be saved, Israel shall dwell in security. This is the name to be given him: “The Lord our justice.”

text.NRSV: Therefore, the days are surely coming, says the Lord, when it shall no longer be said, “As the Lord lives who brought the people of Israel up out of the land of Egypt,”
text.Rotherham: Therefore, lo! days are coming, Declareth Yahweh, When it shall not be said any more As Yahweh liveth, who brought up the sons of Israel out of the land of Egypt;
text.KJV: 23:7 Therefore behold, the dayes come, saith the Lord, that they shall no more say; The Lord liueth, which brought vp the children of Israel out of the land of Egypt:
text.NABRE: Therefore, the days are coming—oracle of the Lord —when they shall no longer say, “As the Lord lives, who brought the Israelites out of the land of Egypt”;

text.NRSV: but “As the Lord lives who brought out and led the offspring of the house of Israel out of the land of the north and out of all the lands where he had driven them.” Then they shall live in their own land.
text.Rotherham: but—As Yahweh liveth who hath brought up and who hath brought in the seed of the house of Israel out of the land of the North, and out of all the lands whither I have driven them,—And they shall remain upon their own soil.
text.KJV: 23:8 But, The Lord liueth, which brought vp, and which led the seed of the house of Israel out of the North countrey, and from all countreis whither I had driuen them, and they shall dwell in their owne land.
text.NABRE: but rather, “As the Lord lives, who brought the descendants of the house of Israel up from the land of the north”—and from all the lands to which I banished them; they shall again live on their own soil.

text.NRSV: Concerning the prophets: My heart is broken within me, all my bones shake; I am like a drunkard, like one overcome by wine, because of the Lord and because of his holy words.
text.Rotherham: As for the prophets,—Broken is my heart within me Trembled have all my bones, I have become as a drunken man, And as a strong man whom wine hath overcome,—Because of Yahweh, And because of his holy words.
text.KJV: 23:9 ¶ Mine heart within me is broken because of the prophets, all my bones shake: I am like a drunken man (and like a man whom wine hath ouercome) because of the Lord, and because of the words of his Holinesse.
text.NABRE: The False Prophets Concerning the prophets: My heart is broken within me, all my bones tremble; I am like a drunk, like one overcome by wine, Because of the Lord, because of his holy words.

text.NRSV: For the land is full of adulterers; because of the curse the land mourns, and the pastures of the wilderness are dried up. Their course has been evil, and their might is not right.
text.Rotherham: For with adulterers, is the land filled, Yea because of cursing, doth the land mourn, Dried up are the oases of the desert,—And, their oppression hath become wicked, And, their might is not right.
text.KJV: 23:10 For the land is full of adulterers, for because of swearing the land mourneth: the pleasant places of the wildernes are dried vp, and their course is euil, and their force is not right.
text.NABRE: The land is filled with adulterers; because of the curse the land mourns, the pastures of the wilderness are withered. Theirs is an evil course, theirs is unjust power.

text.NRSV: Both prophet and priest are ungodly; even in my house I have found their wickedness, says the Lord.
text.Rotherham: For both prophet and priest, are profane,—Even in my house, have I found their wickedness, Declareth Yahweh.
text.KJV: 23:11 For both prophet and priest are prophane, yea in my house haue I found their wickednesse, saith the Lord.
text.NABRE: Both prophet and priest are godless! In my very house I find their wickedness— oracle of the Lord.

text.NRSV: Therefore their way shall be to them like slippery paths in the darkness, into which they shall be driven and fall; for I will bring disaster upon them in the year of their punishment, says the Lord.
text.Rotherham: Therefore, shall their way become to them like slippery places in darkness, They shall be driven on and shall fall therein,—For I will bring in upon them calamity—The year of their visitation, Declareth Yahweh.
text.KJV: 23:12 Wherefore their way shalbe vnto them as slippery wayes in the darkenes: they shalbe driuen on and fall therein: for I will bring euill vpon them, euen the yeere of their visitation, saith the Lord.
text.NABRE: Hence their way shall become for them slippery ground. Into the darkness they shall be driven, and fall headlong; For I will bring disaster upon them, the year of their punishment—oracle of the Lord.

text.NRSV: In the prophets of Samaria I saw a disgusting thing: they prophesied by Baal and led my people Israel astray.
text.Rotherham: Even among the prophets of Samaria, had I seen a foolish thing,—They prophesied by Baal, And led astray my people Israel.
text.KJV: 23:13 And I haue seene folly in the prophets of Samaria; they prophecied in Baal, and caused my people Israel to erre.
text.NABRE: Among Samaria’s prophets I saw something unseemly: They prophesied by Baal and led my people Israel astray.

text.NRSV: But in the prophets of Jerusalem I have seen a more shocking thing: they commit adultery and walk in lies; they strengthen the hands of evildoers, so that no one turns from wickedness; all of them have become like Sodom to me, and its inhabitants like Gomorrah.
text.Rotherham: But among the prophets of Jerusalem, have I seen a horrible thing,—Committing adultery, And walking in falsehood, And so strengthening the hands of doers of wickedness, not to return any man from his wickedness: They have all of them become to me as Sodom, And her inhabitants as Gomorrah.
text.KJV: 23:14 I haue seene also in the prophets of Ierusalem an horrible thing: they commit adultery, and walke in lies: they strengthen also the hands of euill doers, that none doeth returne from his wickednesse: they are all of them vnto me as Sodom, and the inhabitants thereof as Gomorrah.
text.NABRE: But among Jerusalem’s prophets I saw something more shocking: Adultery, walking in deception, strengthening the power of the wicked, so that no one turns from evil; To me they are all like Sodom, its inhabitants like Gomorrah.

text.NRSV: Therefore thus says the Lord of hosts concerning the prophets: “I am going to feed them with wormwood, and give them poisonous water to drink; for from the prophets of Jerusalem ungodliness has gone forth into all the land.”
text.Rotherham: Therefore, Thus, saith Yahweh of hosts Concerning the prophets,—Behold me! Feeding them with wormwood, And I will cause them to drink poisoned water,—For from the prophets of Jerusalem, hath there gone forth profanity unto all the land.
text.KJV: 23:15 Therefore thus saith the Lord of hosts concerning the Prophets; Behold, I will feede them with wormewood, and make them drinke the water of gall: for from the Prophets of Ierusalem is profanenesse gone forth into all the land.
text.NABRE: Therefore, thus says the Lord of hosts against the prophets: Look, I will give them wormwood to eat, and poisoned water to drink; For from Jerusalem’s prophets ungodliness has gone forth into the whole land.

text.NRSV: Thus says the Lord of hosts: Do not listen to the words of the prophets who prophesy to you; they are deluding you. They speak visions of their own minds, not from the mouth of the Lord.
text.Rotherham: Thus, saith Yahweh of hosts,—Do not hearken unto the words of the prophets who are prophesying unto you, They are filling you, with vain hopes,—The vision of their own hearts, do they speak, Not from the mouth of Yahweh!
text.KJV: 23:16 Thus saith the Lord of hosts, Hearken not vnto the wordes of the prophets that prophecie vnto you; they make you vaine: they speake a vision of their owne heart, and not out of the mouth of the Lord.
text.NABRE: Thus says the Lord of hosts: Do not listen to the words of your prophets, who fill you with emptiness; They speak visions from their own fancy, not from the mouth of the Lord.

text.NRSV: They say continually to those who despise the word of the Lord, “It shall be well with you”; and to all who stubbornly follow their own stubborn hearts, they say, “No calamity shall come upon you.”
text.Rotherham: They keep on saying to them who despise me, Yahweh, hath spoken, saying, Prosperity, shall ye have! And to every one who is going on in the stubbornness of his own heart, have they said, There shall come on you, no calamity
text.KJV: 23:17 They say still vnto them that despise me; The Lord hath sayde, Yee shall haue peace; and they say vnto euery one that walketh after the imagination of his owne heart, No euill shall come vpon you.
text.NABRE: They say to those who despise the word of the Lord, “Peace shall be yours”; And to everyone who walks in hardness of heart, “No evil shall overtake you.”

text.NRSV: For who among them has stood in the council of the Lord so as to see and to hear his word? Who has given heed to his word and listened?
text.Rotherham: For who hath stood in the council of Yahweh, that he should see and hear his word? Who hath given ear to his word and heard it?
text.KJV: 23:18 For who hath stood in the counsell of the Lord, and hath perceiued, and heard his word? who hath marked his word, and heard it?
text.NABRE: Now, who has stood in the council of the Lord, to see him and to hear his word? Who has heeded his word so as to announce it?

text.NRSV: Look, the storm of the Lord! Wrath has gone forth, a whirling tempest; it will burst upon the head of the wicked.
text.Rotherham: Lo! the tempest of Yahweh! Indignation, hath come forth, Even a tempest whirling along: On the head of the lawless, shall it hurl itself down.
text.KJV: 23:19 Behold, a whirlewinde of the Lord is gone foorth in furie, euen a grieuous whirlewinde, it shall fall grieuously vpon the head of the wicked.
text.NABRE: See, the storm of the Lord! His wrath breaks forth In a whirling storm that bursts upon the heads of the wicked.

text.NRSV: The anger of the Lord will not turn back until he has executed and accomplished the intents of his mind. In the days to come you will understand it clearly.
text.Rotherham: The anger of Yahweh, wilt not return, Until he hath executed nor Until he hath established The purposes of his heart,—In the afterpart of the days, shall ye understand it perfectly.
text.KJV: 23:20 The anger of the Lord shall not returne, vntill hee haue executed, and til he haue performed the thoughts of his heart: in the latter dayes ye shall consider it perfectly.
text.NABRE: The anger of the Lord shall not abate until he has carried out completely the decisions of his heart. In days to come you will understand fully.

text.NRSV: I did not send the prophets, yet they ran; I did not speak to them, yet they prophesied.
text.Rotherham: I sent not the prophets yet they, ran, I spake not unto them yet, they, prophesied.
text.KJV: 23:21 I haue not sent these prophets, yet they ranne: I haue not spoken to them, yet they prophecied.
text.NABRE: I did not send these prophets, yet they ran; I did not speak to them, yet they prophesied.

text.NRSV: But if they had stood in my council, then they would have proclaimed my words to my people, and they would have turned them from their evil way, and from the evil of their doings.
text.Rotherham: But if they had stood in my council, Then might they have announced my words unto my people, And have turned them from their wicked way and from the wickedness of their doings.
text.KJV: 23:22 But if they had stood in my counsell, and had caused my people to heare my wordes, then they should haue turned them from their euil way, and from the euill of their doings.
text.NABRE: Had they stood in my council, they would have proclaimed my words to my people, They would have brought them back from their evil ways and from their wicked deeds.

text.NRSV: Am I a God near by, says the Lord, and not a God far off?
text.Rotherham: Am I, a God at hand, Demandeth Yahweh, And not a God afar off?
text.KJV: 23:23 Am I a God at hand, sayth the Lord, and not a God afarre off?
text.NABRE: Am I a God near at hand only—oracle of the Lord — and not a God far off?

text.NRSV: Who can hide in secret places so that I cannot see them? says the Lord. Do I not fill heaven and earth? says the Lord.
text.Rotherham: Can any hide himself in secret places that I, shall not see him? Demandeth Yahweh,—The heavens and the earth, do I not fill? Demandeth Yahweh.
text.KJV: 23:24 Can any hide himselfe in secret places that I shall not see him, saith the Lord ? doe not I fill heauen and earth, sayth the Lord ?
text.NABRE: Can anyone hide in secret without my seeing them?—oracle of the Lord. Do I not fill heaven and earth?—oracle of the Lord.

text.NRSV: I have heard what the prophets have said who prophesy lies in my name, saying, “I have dreamed, I have dreamed!”
text.Rotherham: I have heard what the prophets have said, who prophesy in my name falsely, saying,—I have dreamed! I have dreamed!
text.KJV: 23:25 I haue heard what the prophets said, that prophecie lyes in my Name, saying; I haue dreamed, I haue dreamed.
text.NABRE: I have heard the prophets who prophesy lies in my name say, “I had a dream! I had a dream!”

text.NRSV: How long? Will the hearts of the prophets ever turn back—those who prophesy lies, and who prophesy the deceit of their own minds?
text.Rotherham: How long shall it be in the heart of the prophets, To be prophets of falsehood,—And prophets of the deceit of their own heart?
text.KJV: 23:26 How long shall this bee in the heart of the prophets that prophecie lies? yea they are prophets of the deceit of their owne heart;
text.NABRE: How long? Will the hearts of the prophets who prophesy lies and their own deceitful fancies ever turn back?

text.NRSV: They plan to make my people forget my name by their dreams that they tell one another, just as their ancestors forgot my name for Baal.
text.Rotherham: Who lay a plot to cause my people to forget my name by their dreams which they relate every man to his neighbour,—Just as their fathers forgat, my name, for Baal.
text.KJV: 23:27 Which thinke to cause my people to forget my Name by their dreames which they tell euery man to his neighbour, as their fathers haue forgotten my Name, for Baal.
text.NABRE: By the dreams they tell each other, they plan to make my people forget my name, just as their ancestors forgot my name for Baal.

text.NRSV: Let the prophet who has a dream tell the dream, but let the one who has my word speak my word faithfully. What has straw in common with wheat? says the Lord.
text.Rotherham: The prophet with whom is a dream, Let him relate it as a dream, And he with whom is my word, Let him speak my word as truth,—What is the chaff to the wheat? Demandeth Yahweh:
text.KJV: 23:28 The prophet that hath a dreame, let him tell a dreame; and hee that hath my word, let him speake my word faithfully: what is the chaffe to the wheat, sayth the Lord ?
text.NABRE: Let the prophets who have dreams tell their dreams; let those who have my word speak my word truthfully! What has straw to do with wheat? —oracle of the Lord.

text.NRSV: Is not my word like fire, says the Lord, and like a hammer that breaks a rock in pieces?
text.Rotherham: Is not my word, like this, Like fire? Demandeth Yahweh,—And like a hammer, that breaketh in pieces a cliff?
text.KJV: 23:29 Is not my word like as a fire, saith the Lord ? and like a hammer that breaketh the rocke in pieces?
text.NABRE: Is not my word like fire—oracle of the Lord — like a hammer shattering rock?

text.NRSV: See, therefore, I am against the prophets, says the Lord, who steal my words from one another.
text.Rotherham: Therefore! behold me! against the prophets, Declareth Yahweh,—who steal my words, every man from his neighbour:
text.KJV: 23:30 Therefore, behold, I am against the prophets, sayth the Lord, that steale my worde euery one from his neighbour.
text.NABRE: Therefore I am against the prophets—oracle of the Lord —those who steal my words from each other.

text.NRSV: See, I am against the prophets, says the Lord, who use their own tongues and say, “Says the Lord.”
text.Rotherham: Behold me! against the prophets, Declareth Yahweh, who presume with their tongue and declare He declareth:
text.KJV: 23:31 Beholde, I am against the prophets, saith the Lord, that vse their tongues, and say; He sayth.
text.NABRE: Yes, I am against the prophets—oracle of the Lord —those who compose their own speeches and call them oracles.

text.NRSV: See, I am against those who prophesy lying dreams, says the Lord, and who tell them, and who lead my people astray by their lies and their recklessness, when I did not send them or appoint them; so they do not profit this people at all, says the Lord.
text.Rotherham: Behold me! against such as prophesy the dreams of falsehood, Declareth Yahweh, who have related them and led astray my people, with their falsehoods and with their recklessness,—whereas, I, had not sent them nor commanded them, so that they could be of no, profit, to this people, Declareth Yahweh.
text.KJV: 23:32 Behold, I am against them that prophecie false dreames, sayeth the Lord, and doe tell them, and cause my people to erre by their lyes and by their lightnesse, yet I sent them not, nor commanded them: therefore they shall not profite this people at all, sayth the Lord.
text.NABRE: Yes, I am against the prophets who tell lying dreams—oracle of the Lord —those who lead my people astray by recounting their reckless lies. It was not I who sent them or commanded them; they do this people no good at all—oracle of the Lord.

text.NRSV: When this people, or a prophet, or a priest asks you, “What is the burden of the Lord?” you shall say to them, “You are the burden, and I will cast you off, says the Lord.”
text.Rotherham: But when this people or a prophet or a priest shall ask thee saying What is the oracle of Yahweh? Then shalt thou say unto them Ye, yourselves, are the oracle, Therefore will I reject you Declareth Yahweh;
text.KJV: 23:33 ¶ And when this people, or the prophet, or a priest shall aske thee, saying; What is the burden of the Lord ? thou shalt then say vnto them; What burden? I will euen forsake you, saith the Lord.
text.NABRE: And when this people or a prophet or a priest asks you, “What is the burden of the Lord?” you shall answer, “You are the burden, and I cast you off”—oracle of the Lord.

text.NRSV: And as for the prophet, priest, or the people who say, “The burden of the Lord,” I will punish them and their households.
text.Rotherham: But the prophet, or the priest or the people who shall say—the oracle of Yahweh, I will bring punishment upon that man and upon his house.
text.KJV: 23:34 And as for the prophet, and the priest, and the people that shal say, The burden of the Lord, I will euen punish that man and his house.
text.NABRE: If a prophet or a priest or anyone else mentions “the burden of the Lord,” I will punish that man and his household.

text.NRSV: Thus shall you say to one another, among yourselves, “What was the Lord’s answer?” or, “What has the Lord spoken?”
text.Rotherham: Thus, shall ye say—every man unto his neighbour and every man unto his brother,—What hath Yahweh, answered? or, What hath Yahweh, spoken?
text.KJV: 23:35 Thus shall yee say euery one to his neighbour, and euery one to his brother, What hath the Lord answered? and what hath the Lord spoken?
text.NABRE: Thus you shall ask, when speaking to one another, “What answer did the Lord give?” or “What did the Lord say?”

text.NRSV: But “the burden of the Lord” you shall mention no more, for the burden is everyone’s own word, and so you pervert the words of the living God, the Lord of hosts, our God.
text.Rotherham: but the oracle of Yahweh, shall ye not mention, any more,—for, every man’s oracle, shall be his own word, because ye have perverted the words of a Living God, Yahweh of hosts our God.
text.KJV: 23:36 And the burden of the Lord shall yee mention no more: for euery mans word shall be his burden: for yee haue peruerted the words of the liuing God, of the Lord of hostes our God.
text.NABRE: But “the burden of the Lord ” you shall mention no more. For each of you, your own word becomes the burden so that you pervert the words of the living God, the Lord of hosts, our God.

text.NRSV: Thus you shall say to the prophet, “What was the Lord’s answer to you?” or, “What has the Lord spoken?”
text.Rotherham: Thus, shalt thou say unto the prophet,—What hath Yahweh, answered thee? or What hath Yahweh, spoken?
text.KJV: 23:37 Thus shalt thou say to the prophet, What hath the Lord answered thee? and what hath the Lord spoken?
text.NABRE: Thus shall you ask the prophet, “What answer did the Lord give?” or “What did the Lord say?”

text.NRSV: But if you say, “the burden of the Lord,” thus says the Lord: Because you have said these words, “the burden of the Lord,” when I sent to you, saying, You shall not say, “the burden of the Lord,”
text.Rotherham: But, since ye keep on saying, The Oracle of Yahweh, therefore, Thus saith Yahweh, Because ye have said this word, The oracle of Yahweh, whereas I had sent unto you saying, Ye shall not say, The oracle of Yahweh
text.KJV: 23:38 But sith ye say, The burden of the Lord; therefore thus sayeth the Lord, Because you say this word, The burden of the Lord, and I haue sent vnto you, saying; Ye shall not say, The burden of the Lord:
text.NABRE: But if you ask about “the burden of the Lord,” then thus says the Lord: Because you use this phrase, “the burden of the Lord,” though I forbade you to use it,

text.NRSV: therefore, I will surely lift you up and cast you away from my presence, you and the city that I gave to you and your ancestors.
text.Rotherham: therefore, behold me! I will lift you up,—and carry you away, and the city which I gave to you and to your fathers from before my face;
text.KJV: 23:39 Therefore beholde, I, euen I will vtterly forget you, and I will forsake you, and the citie that I gaue you and your fathers, and cast you out of my presence.
text.NABRE: therefore I will lift you on high and cast you from my presence, you and the city which I gave to you and your ancestors.

text.NRSV: And I will bring upon you everlasting disgrace and perpetual shame, which shall not be forgotten.
text.Rotherham: and will give unto you reproach age-abiding,—and disgrace age-abiding, which shall not be forgotten.
text.KJV: 23:40 And I will bring an euerlasting reproch vpon you, and a perpetuall shame, which shall not be forgotten.
text.NABRE: And I will bring upon you eternal reproach, eternal shame, never to be forgotten.

text.NRSV: The Lord showed me two baskets of figs placed before the temple of the Lord. This was after King Nebuchadrezzar of Babylon had taken into exile from Jerusalem King Jeconiah son of Jehoiakim of Judah, together with the officials of Judah, the artisans, and the smiths, and had brought them to Babylon.
text.Rotherham: Yahweh shewed me, and lo! two baskets of figs, which had been set before the temple of Yahweh,—after that Nebuchadrezzar king of Babylon had carried away captive, Jeconiah son of Jehoiakim king of Judah and the princes of Judah and the carpenters and the smiths out of Jerusalem, and had brought them into Babylon:—
text.KJV: 24:1 The Lord shewed mee, and behold, two baskets of figges were set before the temple of the Lord, after that Nebuchad-rezzar king of Babylon had caried away captiue Ieconiah the sonne of Iehoiakim king of Iudah, and the princes of Iudah, with the carpenters and smiths from Ierusalem, and had brought them to Babylon.
text.NABRE: Chapter 24 The Two Baskets of Figs. The Lord showed me two baskets of figs placed before the temple of the Lord. This was after Nebuchadnezzar, king of Babylon, had exiled from Jerusalem Jeconiah, son of Jehoiakim, king of Judah, and the princes of Judah, the artisans and smiths, and brought them to Babylon.

text.NRSV: One basket had very good figs, like first-ripe figs, but the other basket had very bad figs, so bad that they could not be eaten.
text.Rotherham: the one basket, was of very good figs, like the first-ripe figs; and, the other basket, was of very bad figs, which could not be eaten for badness.
text.KJV: 24:2 One basket had very good figges, euen like the figges that are first ripe: and the other basket had very naughty figges, which could not be eaten, they were so bad.
text.NABRE: One basket contained excellent figs, those that ripen early. But the other basket contained very bad figs, so bad they could not be eaten.

text.NRSV: And the Lord said to me, “What do you see, Jeremiah?” I said, “Figs, the good figs very good, and the bad figs very bad, so bad that they cannot be eaten.”
text.Rotherham: Then said Yahweh unto me,—What canst thou see, Jeremiah? And I said, Figs: the good figs, very good; and the bad, very bad, which cannot be eaten, for badness.
text.KJV: 24:3 Then said the Lord vnto me; What seest thou Ieremiah? and I said: Figges: the good figges, very good and the euill, very euill, that cannot be eaten, they are so euill.
text.NABRE: Then the Lord said to me: What do you see, Jeremiah? “Figs,” I replied; “the good ones are very good, but the bad ones very bad, so bad they cannot be eaten.”

text.NRSV: Then the word of the Lord came to me:
text.Rotherham: Then came the word of Yahweh unto me, saying:
text.KJV: 24:4 ¶ Againe, the word of the Lord came vnto me, saying;
text.NABRE: Thereupon this word of the Lord came to me:

text.NRSV: Thus says the Lord, the God of Israel: Like these good figs, so I will regard as good the exiles from Judah, whom I have sent away from this place into the land of the Chaldeans.
text.Rotherham: Thus, saith Yahweh God of Israel, Like these good figs, so, will I regard them of Judah who are carried into captivity, whom I have sent out of this place into the land of the Chaldeans, for good.
text.KJV: 24:5 Thus saith the Lord, the God of Israel, Like these good figges, so will I acknowledge them that are caried away captiue of Iudah, whom I haue sent out of this place into the land of the Caldeans for their good.
text.NABRE: Thus says the Lord, the God of Israel: Like these good figs, I will also regard with favor Judah’s exiles whom I sent away from this place into the land of the Chaldeans.

text.NRSV: I will set my eyes upon them for good, and I will bring them back to this land. I will build them up, and not pull them down; I will plant them, and not pluck them up.
text.Rotherham: Therefore will I set mine eye upon them for good, and will bring them back upon this land,—and will build them up, and not pull them down, and will plant them and not root them up;
text.KJV: 24:6 For I will set mine eyes vpon them for good, and I will bring them againe to this land, and I will build them, and not pull them downe, and I will plant them, and not plucke them vp.
text.NABRE: I will look after them for good and bring them back to this land, to build them up, not tear them down; to plant them, not uproot them.

text.NRSV: I will give them a heart to know that I am the Lord; and they shall be my people and I will be their God, for they shall return to me with their whole heart.
text.Rotherham: and will give them a heart to know, me, that, I, am Yahweh, So shall they become my people, And, I, will become their God; for they will return unto me with all their heart.
text.KJV: 24:7 And I will giue them an heart to know me, that I am the Lord, and they shall be my people, and I will be their God: for they shall returne vnto me with their whole heart.
text.NABRE: I will give them a heart to know me, that I am the Lord. They shall be my people and I will be their God, for they shall return to me with their whole heart.

text.NRSV: But thus says the Lord: Like the bad figs that are so bad they cannot be eaten, so will I treat King Zedekiah of Judah, his officials, the remnant of Jerusalem who remain in this land, and those who live in the land of Egypt.
text.Rotherham: And like the bad figs, which cannot be eaten for badness, Surely thus, saith Yahweh—so, will I deliver up Zedekiah king of Judah, and his princes and the remnant of Jerusalem that remain in this land, and them who are dwelling in the land of Egypt;
text.KJV: 24:8 ¶ And as the euill figges which cannot be eaten, they are so euill; (Surely thus saith the Lord) so will I giue Zedekiah the king of Iudah, and his princes, and the residue of Ierusalem, that remaine in this land, and them that dwell in the land of Egypt.
text.NABRE: But like the figs that are bad, so bad they cannot be eaten—yes, thus says the Lord —even so will I treat Zedekiah, king of Judah, and his princes, the remnant of Jerusalem remaining in this land and those who have settled in the land of Egypt.

text.NRSV: I will make them a shocking sight, an evil, to all the kingdoms of the earth, a reproach, a byword, a taunt, and a curse in all the places where I shall drive them.
text.Rotherham: yea I will deliver them up, as a terror of calamity, to all the kingdoms of the earth,—as a reproach and as a byword, as a mockery and as a contempt, in every place whither I will drive them;
text.KJV: 24:9 And I will deliuer them to be remoued into all the kingdomes of the earth for their hurt, to be a reproch and a prouerbe, a taunt and a curse in all places whither I shall driue them.
text.NABRE: I will make them an object of horror to all the kingdoms of the earth, a reproach and a byword, a taunt and a curse, in all the places to which I will drive them.

text.NRSV: And I will send sword, famine, and pestilence upon them, until they are utterly destroyed from the land that I gave to them and their ancestors.
text.Rotherham: and I will send among them, sword famine and pestilence,—until they are consumed from off the soil which I gave to them and to their fathers.
text.KJV: 24:10 And I will send the sword, the famine, and the pestilence among them, till they be consumed from off the land, that I gaue vnto them, and to their fathers.
text.NABRE: I will send upon them sword, famine, and pestilence, until they have disappeared from the land which I gave them and their ancestors.

text.NRSV: The word that came to Jeremiah concerning all the people of Judah, in the fourth year of King Jehoiakim son of Josiah of Judah (that was the first year of King Nebuchadrezzar of Babylon),
text.Rotherham: The word which came upon Jeremiah concerning all the people of Judah, in the fourth year of Jehoiakim son of Josiah king of Judah,—the same, was the first year of Nebuchadrezzar king of Babylon;
text.KJV: 25:1 The word that came to Ieremiah concerning all the people of Iudah, in the fourth yeere of Iehoiakim the sonne of Iosiah king of Iudah, that was the first yeere of Nebuchad-rezzar king of Babylon:
text.NABRE: Chapter 25 Seventy Years of Exile. The word that came to Jeremiah concerning all the people of Judah, in the fourth year of Jehoiakim, son of Josiah, king of Judah (the first year of Nebuchadnezzar, king of Babylon).

text.NRSV: which the prophet Jeremiah spoke to all the people of Judah and all the inhabitants of Jerusalem:
text.Rotherham: which word Jeremiah the prophet spake concerning all the people of Judah, and against all the inhabitants of Jerusalem saying:—
text.KJV: 25:2 The which Ieremiah the prophet spake vnto all the people of Iudah, and to all the inhabitants of Ierusalem, saying;
text.NABRE: This word the prophet Jeremiah spoke to all the people of Judah and all the inhabitants of Jerusalem:

text.NRSV: For twenty-three years, from the thirteenth year of King Josiah son of Amon of Judah, to this day, the word of the Lord has come to me, and I have spoken persistently to you, but you have not listened.
text.Rotherham: From the thirteenth year of Josiah son of Amon king of Judah even until this day, the which is the twenty-third year, hath the word of Yahweh come unto me; and I have spoken unto you, betimes, speaking, yet have ye not hearkened.
text.KJV: 25:3 From the thirteenth yere of Iosiah the sonne of Amon king of Iudah, euen vnto this day (that is the three and twentith yeere) the word of the Lord hath come vnto me, and I haue spoken vnto you, rising early and speaking, but yee haue not hearkened.
text.NABRE: Since the thirteenth year of Josiah, son of Amon, king of Judah, to this day—that is, twenty-three years—the word of the Lord has come to me and I spoke to you untiringly, but you would not listen.

text.NRSV: And though the Lord has persistently sent all his servants the prophets to you, you have not listened or inclined your ears to hear
text.Rotherham: And Yahweh sent unto you all, his servants the prophets, betimes sending, though ye hearkened not, neither inclined ye your ear to hear:
text.KJV: 25:4 And the Lord hath sent vnto you all his seruants the prophets, rising early and sending them, but yee haue not hearkened, nor inclined your eare to heare.
