text.Rotherham: For seeing thou hast trusted in thy works and in thy treasures, Thou too, shalt be captured,—Then shall Chemosh go forth into captivity, His priests and his princes together.
text.KJV: 48:7 ¶ For because thou hast trusted in thy workes, and in thy treasures, thou shalt also be taken, and Chemosh shall goe foorth into captiuitie with his priests and his princes together.
text.NABRE: Because you trusted in your works and your treasures, you also shall be captured. Chemosh shall go into exile, his priests and princes with him.

text.NRSV: The destroyer shall come upon every town, and no town shall escape; the valley shall perish, and the plain shall be destroyed, as the Lord has spoken.
text.Rotherham: And the spoiler shall enter into every city, And not, a city, shall escape, Then shall perish the vale Then be destroyed the table-land,—As Yahweh hath said.
text.KJV: 48:8 And the spoyler shall come vpon euery citie, and no citie shall escape: the valley also shal perish, & the plaine shall be destroyed, as þ Lord hath spoken.
text.NABRE: The destroyer comes upon every city, not a city escapes; Ruined is the valley, wasted the plateau—oracle of the Lord.

text.NRSV: Set up a tombstone for Moab, for she will surely be laid in ruins; her towns shall become a desolation, with no inhabitant in them.
text.Rotherham: Give wing to Moab, For she must, fly away; And her cities To desolation, shall be turned, With no inhabitant therein.
text.KJV: 48:9 Giue wings vnto Moab, that it may flee and get away: for the cities thereof shalbe desolate, without any to dwell therein.
text.NABRE: Set up a tombstone for Moab; it will soon become a complete wasteland, Its cities turned into ruins where no one dwells.

text.NRSV: Accursed is the one who is slack in doing the Lord’s work, and accursed is the one who keeps back the sword from bloodshed.
text.Rotherham: Accursed be he that doeth the business of Yahweh carelessly; And, accursed be he that withholdeth his sword, from blood!
text.KJV: 48:10 Cursed be he that doeth the worke of the Lord deceitfully, and cursed be he that keepeth backe his sword from blood.
text.NABRE: Cursed are they who do the Lord ’s work carelessly, cursed those who keep their sword from shedding blood.

text.NRSV: Moab has been at ease from his youth, settled like wine on its dregs; he has not been emptied from vessel to vessel, nor has he gone into exile; therefore his flavor has remained and his aroma is unchanged.
text.Rotherham: Moab, hath been at ease from his youth, And settled is he upon his lees, And hath not been poured out from vessel to vessel, Nor into captivity, hath gone,—For this cause, hath his flavour stood still within him, And his scent is unchanged.
text.KJV: 48:11 ¶ Moab hath bene at ease from his youth, and hee hath setled on his lees, and hath not been emptied from vessell to vessell, neither hath he gone into captiuitie: therefore his taste remained in him, and his sent is not changed.
text.NABRE: Moab has been resting from its youth, suspended above its dregs, Never poured from flask to flask, never driven into exile. Thus it retained its flavor, its bouquet is not lost.

text.NRSV: Therefore, the days are surely coming, says the Lord, when I shall send to him decanters to tip him over, and empty his vessels, and break his jars in pieces.
text.Rotherham: Therefore, lo! days are coming, Declareth Yahweh, When I will send to him tilters and they shall tilt him,—And his vessels, shall they empty, And their jars, shall they dash in pieces.
text.KJV: 48:12 Therfore behold, the dayes come, sayth the Lord, that I will send vnto him wanderers that shall cause him to wander, and shall emptie his vessels, and breake their bottles.
text.NABRE: Be assured! The days are coming—oracle of the Lord —when I will send him wine-makers to decant the wine; they shall empty its flasks and smash its jars.

text.NRSV: Then Moab shall be ashamed of Chemosh, as the house of Israel was ashamed of Bethel, their confidence.
text.Rotherham: Then shall Moab, be ashamed, of, Chemosh,—Just as, the sons of Israel, were ashamed of Bethel their confidence.
text.KJV: 48:13 And Moab shall bee ashamed of Chemosh, as the house of Israel was ashamed of Bethel their confidence.
text.NABRE: Chemosh shall disappoint Moab, just as the house of Israel was disappointed by Bethel, in which they trusted.

text.NRSV: How can you say, “We are heroes and mighty warriors”?
text.Rotherham: How can ye say, Heroes, are we? and Men of might for the war?
text.KJV: 48:14 ¶ How say yee, We are mightie and strong men for the warre?
text.NABRE: How can you say, “We are heroes, mighty warriors”?

text.NRSV: The destroyer of Moab and his towns has come up, and the flower of his young men has gone down to slaughter, says the King, whose name is the Lord of hosts.
text.Rotherham: Spoiled is Moab And her cities, hath he ascended, And the choice of his young men, have gone down to the slaughter,—Declareth The King, Yahweh of hosts, is his Name.
text.KJV: 48:15 Moab is spoiled and gone vp out of her cities, and his chosen yong men are gone downe to the slaughter, sayth the King, whose Name is the Lord of hostes.
text.NABRE: The one who ravages Moab and its cities comes up, the best of its youth go down to slaughter— oracle of the King, whose name is Lord of hosts.

text.NRSV: The calamity of Moab is near at hand, and his doom approaches swiftly.
text.Rotherham: Near is the doom of Moab, to come,—And, his calamity, hasteth greatly.
text.KJV: 48:16 The calamitie of Moab is neere to come, and his affliction hasteth fast.
text.NABRE: Moab’s ruin is near at hand, its disaster approaches swiftly.

text.NRSV: Mourn over him, all you his neighbors, and all who know his name; say, “How the mighty scepter is broken, the glorious staff!”
text.Rotherham: Lament ye for him—All that are round about him, and All that know his name,—Say, How is broken—The staff of strength! The rod of beauty!
text.KJV: 48:17 All yee that are about him bemoane him, and all yee that know his Name, say, How is the strong staffe broken, and the beautifull rod!
text.NABRE: Mourn, all you neighbors, all you who know its name! Say: How the mighty scepter is broken, the glorious staff!

text.NRSV: Come down from glory, and sit on the parched ground, enthroned daughter Dibon! For the destroyer of Moab has come up against you; he has destroyed your strongholds.
text.Rotherham: Come down from glory, and sit in thirst, O inhabitress daughter of Dibon; For the spoiler of Moab, hath come up against thee, He hath laid in ruins thy strongholds.
text.KJV: 48:18 Thou daughter that doest inhabit Dibon, come downe from thy glory, and sit in thirst; for the spoiler of Moab shall come vpon thee, and he shall destroy thy strong holdes.
text.NABRE: Come down from glory, sit on the parched ground, enthroned daughter Dibon; Moab’s destroyer has fallen upon you, has shattered your strongholds.

text.NRSV: Stand by the road and watch, you inhabitant of Aroer! Ask the man fleeing and the woman escaping; say, “What has happened?”
text.Rotherham: Near the way, take thy stand and keep outlook, O inhabitress of Aroer: Ask—him that fleeth, and Her that hath escaped, Say, What hath happened?
text.KJV: 48:19 O inhabitant of Aroer, stand by the way and espie, aske him that fleeth, and her that escapeth, and say, What is done?
text.NABRE: Stand along the road, keep watch, enthroned Aroer; Ask the fleeing man, the escaping woman: ask them what has happened.

text.NRSV: Moab is put to shame, for it is broken; wail and cry! Tell it by the Arnon, that Moab is laid waste.
text.Rotherham: Confounded is Moab! For it is broken down! Howl and make outcry,—Tell ye in Arnon, That Moab is spoiled;
text.KJV: 48:20 Moab is confounded, for it is broken downe: howle and cry, tell ye it in Arnon, that Moab is spoiled,
text.NABRE: “Moab is put to shame, destroyed.” Wail and cry out, Proclaim it at the Arnon: “Moab is destroyed!”

text.NRSV: Judgment has come upon the tableland, upon Holon, and Jahzah, and Mephaath,
text.Rotherham: And, judgment, hath come Upon the country of the table-land,—Upon Holon, and Upon Jazer and Upon Mephaath;
text.KJV: 48:21 And iudgement is come vpon the plaine countrey, vpon Holon, and vpon Iahazah, and vpon Mephaath,
text.NABRE: Judgment has come upon the plateau: on Holon, Jahzah, and Mephaath,

text.NRSV: and Dibon, and Nebo, and Beth-diblathaim,
text.Rotherham: and Upon Dibon, and Upon Nebo, and Upon Beth-diblathaim;
text.KJV: 48:22 And vpon Dibon, and vpon Nebo, and vpon Beth-diblathaim,
text.NABRE: on Dibon, Nebo, and Beth-diblathaim,

text.NRSV: and Kiriathaim, and Beth-gamul, and Beth-meon,
text.Rotherham: and Upon Kiriathaim, and Upon Beth-gamul, and Upon Beth-meon;
text.KJV: 48:23 And vpon Kiriathaim, and vpon Beth-Gamul, and vpon Beth-meon,
text.NABRE: on Kiriathaim, Beth-gamul, and Beth-meon,

text.NRSV: and Kerioth, and Bozrah, and all the towns of the land of Moab, far and near.
text.Rotherham: and Upon Kerioth, and Upon Bozrah,—Yea, upon all the cities of the land of Moab, far and near.
text.KJV: 48:24 And vpon Kerioth, and vpon Bozrah, and vpon all the cities of the land of Moab farre or neere.
text.NABRE: on Kerioth and on Bozrah: on all the cities of the land of Moab, far and near.

text.NRSV: The horn of Moab is cut off, and his arm is broken, says the Lord.
text.Rotherham: Cut off is the horn of Moab, Yea, his arm, is broken,—Declareth Yahweh.
text.KJV: 48:25 The horne of Moab is cut off, & his arme is broken, saith the Lord.
text.NABRE: The horn of Moab is cut off, its arm is broken—oracle of the Lord.

text.NRSV: Make him drunk, because he magnified himself against the Lord; let Moab wallow in his vomit; he too shall be held in derision.
text.Rotherham: Make ye him drunken, Because against Yahweh, hath he magnified himself; So shall, Moab, stagger, into his own vomit, And shall become a derision, even he!
text.KJV: 48:26 ¶ Make ye him drunken: for hee magnified himselfe against the Lord: Moab also shall wallow in his vomit, and he also shalbe in derision.
text.NABRE: Make him drunk because he set himself over against the Lord; let Moab swim in his vomit and become a laughingstock.

text.NRSV: Was not Israel a derision to you? Was he found among thieves, that whenever you spoke of him you shook your head?
text.Rotherham: And was it not, a derision, that Israel became to thee? And was it not among thieves, he was found? For, as often as thou didst speak of him, thou didst wag the head!
text.KJV: 48:27 For was not Israel a derision vnto thee? was hee found among theeues? for since thou spakest of him, thou skippedst for ioy.
text.NABRE: Has Israel not been a laughingstock to you? Was he caught among thieves that you wag your heads whenever you speak of him?

text.NRSV: Leave the towns, and live on the rock, O inhabitants of Moab! Be like the dove that nests on the sides of the mouth of a gorge.
text.Rotherham: Leave ye the cities and dwell in the cliff, Ye inhabitants of Moab; And become ye as a dove, that maketh her nest in the further-side of the fissure’s mouth.
text.KJV: 48:28 O yee that dwell in Moab, leaue the cities and dwell in the rocke, and be like the doue that maketh her nest in the sides of the holes mouth.
text.NABRE: Abandon the cities, take shelter in the crags, inhabitants of Moab. Be like the dove that nests in the walls of a gorge.

text.NRSV: We have heard of the pride of Moab— he is very proud!— of his loftiness, his pride, and his arrogance, and the haughtiness of his heart.
text.Rotherham: We have heard the arrogance of Moab—proud exceedingly: His loftiness and his arrogance and his majesty, and his elevation of heart!
text.KJV: 48:29 We haue heard the pride of Moab, (he is exceeding proud) his loftinesse and his arrogancie, and his pride, and the hautinesse of his heart.
text.NABRE: We have heard of the pride of Moab, pride beyond bounds: His loftiness, his pride, his scorn, his insolent heart.

text.NRSV: I myself know his insolence, says the Lord; his boasts are false, his deeds are false.
text.Rotherham: I know—Declareth Yahweh His passion—that ’tis Unjust! His boastings, that Untrue have they made [them] !
text.KJV: 48:30 I knowe his wrath, sayeth the Lord, but it shall not be so, his lyes shall not so effect it.
text.NABRE: I myself know his arrogance—oracle of the Lord — liar in word, liar in deed.

text.NRSV: Therefore I wail for Moab; I cry out for all Moab; for the men of Kir-heres I mourn.
text.Rotherham: For this cause over Moab, will I howl, And for all Moab, will I make outcry,—For the men of Kir-heres, must one moan!
text.KJV: 48:31 Therefore will I howle for Moab, and I will cry out for all Moab, mine heart shall mourne for the men of Kir-heres.
text.NABRE: And so I wail over Moab, over all Moab I cry, over the people of Kir-heres I moan.

text.NRSV: More than for Jazer I weep for you, O vine of Sibmah! Your branches passed over the sea, they reached as far as Jazer; upon your summer fruits and your vintage the destroyer has fallen.
text.Rotherham: Beyond the weeping of Jazer, will I weep for thee O vine of Sibmah, Thy tendrils, have gone over the sea, Unto the sea of Jazer, have they reached, Upon thy summer fruits And upon thy vintage, the spoiler, hath fallen.
text.KJV: 48:32 O vine of Sibmah, I wil weepe for thee, with the weeping of Iazer; thy plants are gone ouer the sea, they reach euen to the sea of Iazer, the spoiler is fallen vpon thy summer fruits, and vpon thy vintage.
text.NABRE: More than for Jazer I weep for you, vine of Sibmah. Your tendrils trailed down to the sea, as far as Jazer they stretched. Upon your summer harvest and your vintage, the destroyer has fallen.

text.NRSV: Gladness and joy have been taken away from the fruitful land of Moab; I have stopped the wine from the wine presses; no one treads them with shouts of joy; the shouting is no shout of joy.
text.Rotherham: So shall be withdrawn gladness and exultation—From the fruitful field and From the land of Moab,—And wine from the vats, have I caused to fail, They shall not tread with shouting, The shouting shall be no shouting!
text.KJV: 48:33 And ioy and gladnesse is taken from the plentifull field, and from the land of Moab, and I haue caused wine to faile from the winepresses, none shall tread with shouting, their showting shall be no showting.
text.NABRE: Joy and gladness are taken away from the garden land, the land of Moab. I dry up the wine from the wine vats, the treader treads no more, the vintage shout is stilled.

text.NRSV: Heshbon and Elealeh cry out; as far as Jahaz they utter their voice, from Zoar to Horonaim and Eglath-shelishiyah. For even the waters of Nimrim have become desolate.
text.Rotherham: From the outcry of Heshbon, Even unto Elealeh Even unto Jahaz, have they given forth their voice, From Zoar even unto Horonaim, the third Eglath,—For, even the waters of Nimrim, to utter desolation, have been turned:
text.KJV: 48:34 From the cry of Heshbon euen vnto Elealeh, and euen vnto Iahaz haue they vttered their voyce, from Zoar euen vnto Horonaim as an heifer of three yeeres old: for the waters also of Nimrim shall be desolate.
text.NABRE: The cry of Heshbon and Elealeh is heard as far as Jahaz; they call from Zoar to Horonaim and to Eglath-shelishiyah; even the waters of Nimrim turn into a wasteland.

text.NRSV: And I will bring to an end in Moab, says the Lord, those who offer sacrifice at a high place and make offerings to their gods.
text.Rotherham: Then will I cause to cease, from Moab, Declareth Yahweh,—him that offereth at a high-place, and him that burneth incense to his gods.
text.KJV: 48:35 Moreouer, I will cause to cease in Moab, saith the Lord, him that offereth in the high places, and him that burneth incense to his Gods.
text.NABRE: I will leave no one in Moab—oracle of the Lord —to offer burnt offerings on the high place or to make sacrifices to their gods.

text.NRSV: Therefore my heart moans for Moab like a flute, and my heart moans like a flute for the men of Kir-heres; for the riches they gained have perished.
text.Rotherham: For this cause, my heart, for Moab, like flutes, shall make plaintive sounds, Yea my heart for the men of Kir-heres! like flutes, shall make plaintive sounds, Because, the abundance he hath gotten is lost!
text.KJV: 48:36 Therefore mine heart shall sound for Moab like pipes, and mine heart shall sound like pipes for the men of Kir-heres: because the riches that hee hath gotten is perished.
text.NABRE: Hence my heart wails like a flute for Moab; my heart wails like a flute for the people of Kir-heres: the wealth they accumulated has perished.

text.NRSV: For every head is shaved and every beard cut off; on all the hands are gashes, and on the loins is sackcloth.
text.Rotherham: For, every head, is baldness, And every beard, clipped: Upon all hands, are gashes, And upon the loins, sackcloth
text.KJV: 48:37 For euery head shall be bald, and euery beard clipt: vpon all the hands shall be cuttings, and vpon the loines sackcloth.
text.NABRE: Every head has been shaved bald, every beard cut off; every hand gashed, and all their loins are draped in sackcloth.

text.NRSV: On all the housetops of Moab and in the squares there is nothing but lamentation; for I have broken Moab like a vessel that no one wants, says the Lord.
text.Rotherham: Upon all the housetops of Moab And in her broadways, it is all lamentation,—For I have broken Moab—Like a vessel wherein is no pleasure Declareth Yahweh.
text.KJV: 48:38 There shall be lamentation generally vpon all the house toppes of Moab, and in the streetes thereof: for I haue broken Moab like a vessell, wherin is no pleasure, saith the Lord.
text.NABRE: On all the rooftops of Moab and in all its squares there is mourning. I have shattered Moab like a pot that no one wants—oracle of the Lord.

text.NRSV: How it is broken! How they wail! How Moab has turned his back in shame! So Moab has become a derision and a horror to all his neighbors.
text.Rotherham: How it is broken down! They have howled, How hath Moab turned the back, for shame,—So shall Moab become a derision and a terror to all round about him.
text.KJV: 48:39 They shall howle, saying; How is it broken downe? how hath Moab turned the backe with shame? so shall Moab be a derision, and a dismaying to all them about him.
text.NABRE: How terrified they are, how they wail! How Moab turns its back in shame! Moab has become a laughingstock and a horror to all its neighbors!

text.NRSV: For thus says the Lord: Look, he shall swoop down like an eagle, and spread his wings against Moab;
text.Rotherham: For, thus, saith Yahweh, Lo! as an eagle, shall one dart along,—And shall spread out his wings towards Moab.
text.KJV: 48:40 For thus saith the Lord, Behold, hee shall fly as an eagle, and shall spread his wings ouer Maob.
text.NABRE: For thus says the Lord: Look there! Like an eagle he swoops, spreading his wings over Moab.

text.NRSV: the towns shall be taken and the strongholds seized. The heart of the warriors of Moab on that day shall be like the heart of a woman in labor.
text.Rotherham: Captured is Kerioth, And the fortresses, are seized,—Then shall the heart of the heroes of Moab become in that day, As the heart of a woman in her pains.
text.KJV: 48:41 Kerioth is taken, and the strong holds are surprised, & the mighty mens hearts in Moab at that day shall be as the heart of a woman in her pangs.
text.NABRE: Cities are captured, strongholds seized: On that day the hearts of Moab’s warriors become like the heart of a woman in labor.

text.NRSV: Moab shall be destroyed as a people, because he magnified himself against the Lord.
text.Rotherham: Then shall, Moab, be destroyed from being a people,—Because against Yahweh, hath he magnified himself.
text.KJV: 48:42 And Moab shall be destroyed from being a people, because he hath magnified himselfe against the Lord.
text.NABRE: Moab shall be wiped out, a people no more, because it set itself over against the Lord.

text.NRSV: Terror, pit, and snare are before you, O inhabitant of Moab! says the Lord.
text.Rotherham: Terror and pit and snare are upon thee O inhabitant of Moab Declareth Yahweh:
text.KJV: 48:43 Feare, and the pit, and the snare shall be vpon thee, O inhabitant of Moab, saith the Lord.
text.NABRE: Terror, pit, and trap be upon you, enthroned Moab—oracle of the Lord.

text.NRSV: Everyone who flees from the terror shall fall into the pit, and everyone who climbs out of the pit shall be caught in the snare. For I will bring these things upon Moab in the year of their punishment, says the Lord.
text.Rotherham: He that fleeth from the face of the terror, Shall fall into the pit, And, he that getteth up out of the pit, Shall be captured by the snare,—For I will bring against her—against Moab, The year of their visitation, Declareth Yahweh.
text.KJV: 48:44 Hee that fleeth from the feare shall fall into the pit, and he that getteth vp out of the pit shall be taken in the snare: for I will bring vpon it, euen vpon Moab, the yeere of their visitation, saith the Lord.
text.NABRE: Those fleeing the terror fall into the pit; Those climbing out of the pit are caught in the trap; Ah, yes! I will bring these things upon Moab in the year of their punishment—oracle of the Lord.

text.NRSV: In the shadow of Heshbon fugitives stop exhausted; for a fire has gone out from Heshbon, a flame from the house of Sihon; it has destroyed the foreheads of Moab, the scalps of the tumultuous people.
text.Rotherham: In the shadow of Heshbon, stand strengthless, the fugitives,—For, a fire, hath gone forth out of Heshbon And shall flame out of the midst of Sihon, And shall devour the beard of Moab, And the crown of the head of the proudly tumultuous.
text.KJV: 48:45 They that fled, stood vnder the shadow of Heshbon, because of the force: but a fire shall come forth out of Heshbon, and a flame from the middest of Sihon, and shall deuoure the corner of Moab, and the crowne of the head of the tumultuous ones.
text.NABRE: In Heshbon’s shadow the fugitives stop short, exhausted; For fire blazes up from Heshbon, and flames up from the house of Sihon: It consumes the forehead of Moab, the scalp of the noisemakers.

text.NRSV: Alas for you, O Moab! The people of Chemosh have perished, for your sons have been taken captive, and your daughters into exile.
text.Rotherham: Woe to thee Moab! Lost are the people of Chemosh,—For thy sons have been taken, into captivity, And thy daughters into captivity.
text.KJV: 48:46 Woe be vnto thee, O Moab, the people of Chemosh perisheth: for thy sonnes are taken captiues, and thy daughters captiues.
text.NABRE: Woe to you, Moab! You are finished, people of Chemosh! Your sons are taken into exile, your daughters into captivity.

text.NRSV: Yet I will restore the fortunes of Moab in the latter days, says the Lord. Thus far is the judgment on Moab.
text.Rotherham: Yet will I bring back the captivity of Moab, In the afterpart of the days, Declareth Yahweh, Thus far, is the sentence of Moab.
text.KJV: 48:47 ¶ Yet will I bring againe the captiuitie of Moab in the later dayes, saith the Lord. Thus farre is the iudgement of Moab.
text.NABRE: Yet I will restore the fortunes of Moab in the days to come—oracle of the Lord. Thus far the judgment on Moab.

text.NRSV: Concerning the Ammonites. Thus says the Lord: Has Israel no sons? Has he no heir? Why then has Milcom dispossessed Gad, and his people settled in its towns?
text.Rotherham: Of the sons of Ammon-Thus saith Yahweh: Hath Israel no, sons? Hath he no, heir? Why hath, Malkam, taken possession of Gad, Or have, his people, in the cities thereof, made their dwelling?
text.KJV: 49:1 Concerning the Ammonites, thus sayth the Lord; hath Israel no sonnes? Hath he no heire? Why then doth their king inherit God, and his people dwell in his cities?
text.NABRE: Chapter 49 Against the Ammonites. Concerning the Ammonites. Thus says the Lord: Has Israel no sons? none to inherit? Why has Milcom disinherited Gad, why are his people living in its cities?

text.NRSV: Therefore, the days are surely coming, says the Lord, when I will cause the battle cry to be heard against Rabbah of the Ammonites; it shall become a desolate mound, and its villages shall be burned with fire; then Israel shall dispossess those who dispossessed him, says the Lord.
text.Rotherham: Therefore, lo! days are coming, Declareth Yahweh When I will cause to be heard against Rabbah of the sons of Ammon, an alarm of war, So shall she become a mound of desolation, And, her villages, with fire, shall blaze,—Then shall Israel, inherit them who inherited him, Saith Yahweh.
text.KJV: 49:2 Therfore behold, the dayes come, saith the Lord, that I will cause an alarme of warre to be heard in Rabbah of the Ammonites, and it shall be a desolate heape, and her daughters shall be burnt with fire: then shall Israel be heire vnto them that were his heires, saith the Lord.
text.NABRE: Therefore the days are coming—oracle of the Lord — when I will sound the battle alarm against Rabbah of the Ammonites; It shall become a mound of ruins, and its villages destroyed by fire. Israel shall then inherit those who disinherited it— oracle of the Lord.

text.NRSV: Wail, O Heshbon, for Ai is laid waste! Cry out, O daughters of Rabbah! Put on sackcloth, lament, and slash yourselves with whips! For Milcom shall go into exile, with his priests and his attendants.
text.Rotherham: Howl, O Heshbon, for Ai is spoiled Make outcry, ye daughters of Rabbah, Gird you with sackcloth, Lament ye and run to and fro among the fences,—For, Malkam, into captivity, shall go, His priests and his princes, together.
text.KJV: 49:3 Howle, O Heshbon, for Ai is spoiled: cry yee daughters of Rabbah, gird yee with sackcloth: lament and runne to and fro by the hedges: for their king shall goe into captiuitie: and his priests and his princes together.
text.NABRE: Wail, Heshbon, “The ruin is destroyed!” shriek, villages of Rabbah! Put on sackcloth and lament! Run back and forth in the sheepfolds. For Milcom is going into exile, taking priest and prince with him.

text.NRSV: Why do you boast in your strength? Your strength is ebbing, O faithless daughter. You trusted in your treasures, saying, “Who will dare to attack me?”
text.Rotherham: Why shouldst thou glory in the vales, The flowing of thy vale O apostate daughter? She who is trusting in her treasures, [saying,] Who shall invade, me?
text.KJV: 49:4 Wherfore gloriest thou in the valleys, thy flowing valley, O backsliding daughter? That trusted in her treasures, saying; Who shall come vnto mee?
text.NABRE: Why boast in your strength, your ebbing strength, rebellious daughter? Why trust in your treasures, saying, “Who would dare attack me?”

text.NRSV: I am going to bring terror upon you, says the Lord God of hosts, from all your neighbors, and you will be scattered, each headlong, with no one to gather the fugitives.
text.Rotherham: Behold me! bringing upon thee, terror Declareth My Lord, Yahweh of hosts, From all who are round about thee,—And ye shall be driven out every man before it, And there shall be none to bring home the wanderer;
text.KJV: 49:5 Behold, I will bring a feare vpon thee, saith the Lord God of hostes, from all those that be about thee, and yee shall be driuen out euery man right forth, and none shal gather vp him that wandereth.
text.NABRE: See, I am bringing terror upon you— oracle of the Lord God of hosts— from all around you; You shall be scattered, each in headlong flight, with no one to gather the fugitives.

text.NRSV: But afterward I will restore the fortunes of the Ammonites, says the Lord.
text.Rotherham: But afterwards, will I bring back the captivity of the sons of Ammon, Declareth Yahweh.
text.KJV: 49:6 And afterward I will bring againe the captiuitie of the children of Ammon, saith the Lord.
text.NABRE: But afterward I will restore the fortunes of the Ammonites—oracle of the Lord. Against Edom.

text.NRSV: Concerning Edom. Thus says the Lord of hosts: Is there no longer wisdom in Teman? Has counsel perished from the prudent? Has their wisdom vanished?
text.Rotherham: Of Edom—Thus, saith Yahweh of hosts, Is there no longer wisdom in Teman? Hath counsel perished from the discerning? Is their wisdom corrupt?
text.KJV: 49:7 ¶ Concerning Edom thus saith the Lord of hosts, Is wisedome no more in Teman? is counsell perished from the prudent? is their wisedom vanished?
text.NABRE: Concerning Edom. Thus says the Lord of hosts: Is there no more wisdom in Teman, has counsel perished from the prudent, is their wisdom gone?

text.NRSV: Flee, turn back, get down low, inhabitants of Dedan! For I will bring the calamity of Esau upon him, the time when I punish him.
text.Rotherham: Flee ye turn go down deep to dwell, O inhabitant of Dedan,—For the doom of Esau, have I brought in upon him The time of his visitation.
text.KJV: 49:8 Flee ye, turne backe, dwell deepe, O inhabitants of Dedan: for I will bring the calamitie of Esau vpon him, the time that I will visite him.
text.NABRE: Flee, retreat, hide deep for lodging, inhabitants of Dedan: For I bring disaster upon Esau when I come to punish them.

text.NRSV: If grape-gatherers came to you, would they not leave gleanings? If thieves came by night, even they would pillage only what they wanted.
text.Rotherham: If, grape-gatherers, came to thee, Would they not leave, gleanings? If, thieves in the night, Would they not have taken away what sufficed them?
text.KJV: 49:9 If grape gatherers come to thee, would they not leaue some gleaning grapes? If theeues by night, they will destroy till they haue enough.
text.NABRE: If vintagers came upon you, they would leave no gleanings; If thieves by night, they would destroy as they pleased.

text.NRSV: But as for me, I have stripped Esau bare, I have uncovered his hiding places, and he is not able to conceal himself. His offspring are destroyed, as are his kinsfolk and his neighbors; and he is no more.
text.Rotherham: But, I, have stripped Esau bare, I have uncovered his secret places, And to conceal himself, shall he not be able! Spoiled, are his seed, and his brethren, and his neighbours, and he is not.
text.KJV: 49:10 But I haue made Esau bare, I haue vncouered his secret places, and he shall not be able to hide himselfe: his seed is spoiled, and his brethren and his neighbours, and he is not.
text.NABRE: So I myself will strip Esau; I will uncover his lairs so he cannot hide. Offspring and family are destroyed, neighbors, too; he is no more.

text.NRSV: Leave your orphans, I will keep them alive; and let your widows trust in me.
text.Rotherham: Leave thy fatherless children I, will preserve them alive,—And thy widows, In me, let them trust.
text.KJV: 49:11 Leaue thy fatherlesse children, I will preserue them aliue: and let thy widowes trust in me.
text.NABRE: Leave your orphans behind, I will keep them alive; your widows, let them trust in me.

text.NRSV: For thus says the Lord: If those who do not deserve to drink the cup must drink it, will you be the one to go unpunished? You shall not go unpunished; you must drink it.
text.Rotherham: For, thus saith Yahweh—Lo! they who had not been adjudged to drink the cup, shall surely drink, And art thou, the one to go unpunished? Thou shalt not go unpunished, but shalt surely drink.
text.KJV: 49:12 For thus saith the Lord, Behold, they whose iudgement was not to drinke of the cup, haue assuredly drunken, and art thou he that shall altogether go vnpunished? thou shalt not go vnpunished, but thou shalt surely drinke of it.
text.NABRE: For thus says the Lord: Look, even those not sentenced to drink the cup must drink it! Shall you then go unpunished? You shall not! You shall drink every bit of it!

text.NRSV: For I have sworn by myself, says the Lord, that Bozrah shall become an object of horror and ridicule, a waste, and an object of cursing; and all her towns shall be perpetual wastes.
text.Rotherham: For by myself, have I sworn, Declareth Yahweh, That, Bozrah shall become, an astonishment: a reproach and a desolation, and a contempt,—And all her cities shall become age-abiding desolations.
text.KJV: 49:13 For I haue sworne by my selfe, saith the Lord, that Bozrah shall become a desolation, a reproch, a waste, and a curse, and all the cities thereof shall be perpetuall wastes.
text.NABRE: By myself I have sworn—oracle of the Lord —Bozrah shall become an object of horror, a disgrace, a desolation, and a curse. Bozrah and all its cities shall become ruins forever.

text.NRSV: I have heard tidings from the Lord, and a messenger has been sent among the nations: “Gather yourselves together and come against her, and rise up for battle!”
text.Rotherham: A report, have I heard from Yahweh, Yea, an envoy, throughout the nations, hath been sent,—Gather yourselves together and come against her, And arise to the battle.
text.KJV: 49:14 I haue heard a rumor from the Lord, & an ambassadour is sent vnto the heathen, saying, Gather ye together & come against her, & rise vp to the battell.
text.NABRE: I have heard a report from the Lord, a herald has been sent among the nations: “Gather together, move against it, get ready for battle!”

text.NRSV: For I will make you least among the nations, despised by humankind.
text.Rotherham: For lo! Small, have I made thee among the nations,—Despised among men!
text.KJV: 49:15 For lo, I wil make thee smal among the heathen, and despised among men.
text.NABRE: I will make you the least among the nations, despised by all people!

text.NRSV: The terror you inspire and the pride of your heart have deceived you, you who live in the clefts of the rock, who hold the height of the hill. Although you make your nest as high as the eagle’s, from there I will bring you down, says the Lord.
text.Rotherham: Thy monstrous thing, hath deceived thee, The insolence of thy heart, O thou that inhabitest the hidden recesses of the cliff, That holdest fast the height of the hill,—Though thou set high as an eagle, thy nest, From thence, will I bring thee down, Declareth Yahweh;
text.KJV: 49:16 Thy terriblenesse hath deceiued thee, and the pride of thine heart, O thou that dwellest in the clefts of the rocke, that holdest the height of the hill: thogh thou shouldest make thy nest as high as the eagle, I will bring thee downe from thence, saith the Lord.
text.NABRE: The terror you spread, the pride of your heart, beguiled you. You denizens of rocks and crevices, occupying towering peaks: Though you build your nest high as the eagle, from there I will bring you down—oracle of the Lord.

text.NRSV: Edom shall become an object of horror; everyone who passes by it will be horrified and will hiss because of all its disasters.
text.Rotherham: So shall, Edom, become an astonishment,—Every one passing by her, will be astonished and hiss, over all her plagues:
text.KJV: 49:17 Also Edom shalbe a desolation: euery one þ goeth by it shalbe astonished, and shall hisse at all the plagues thereof,
text.NABRE: Edom shall become an object of horror. Passersby recoil in terror, hissing at all its wounds.

text.NRSV: As when Sodom and Gomorrah and their neighbors were overthrown, says the Lord, no one shall live there, nor shall anyone settle in it.
text.Rotherham: Like the overthrow of Sodom and Gomorrah and her neighbours, Saith Yahweh,—There shall not dwell there—a man, Nor sojourn therein—any son of the earth-born.
text.KJV: 49:18 As in the ouerthrow of Sodom and Gomorrah, and the neighbour cities thereof, saith the Lord: no man shall abide there, neither shall a sonne of man dwell in it.
text.NABRE: As when Sodom, Gomorrah, and their neighbors were overthrown—oracle of the Lord —no one shall live in it, nor anyone settle there.

text.NRSV: Like a lion coming up from the thickets of the Jordan against a perennial pasture, I will suddenly chase Edom away from it; and I will appoint over it whomever I choose. For who is like me? Who can summon me? Who is the shepherd who can stand before me?
text.Rotherham: Lo! like a lion, will he come up from the majesty of the Jordan, unto the pasture perennial, But I will wink—I will make him run away therefrom, Who then shall be the Chosen One whom over it, I may set in charge? For who shall be like unto, me? And who shall appoint me, a time? And who is the Shepherd that shall stand before, me?
text.KJV: 49:19 Behold, he shal come vp like a lyon from the swelling of Iordane against the habitation of the strong: but I wil suddenly make him runne away from her, and who is a chosen man that I may appoynt ouer her? For who is like mee? And who will appoint me the time? Who is that shepheard that will stand before mee?
text.NABRE: As when a lion comes up from a thicket of the Jordan to a permanent pasture, So in an instant, I will chase them off; I will establish there whomever I choose. For who is like me? Who holds me accountable? What shepherd can stand against me?

text.NRSV: Therefore hear the plan that the Lord has made against Edom and the purposes he has formed against the inhabitants of Teman: Surely the little ones of the flock shall be dragged away; surely their fold shall be appalled at their fate.
text.Rotherham: Wherefore, hear ye The counsel of Yahweh which he hath counseled against Edom, And his devices which he hath devised against the inhabitants of Teman,—Surely the little ones of the flock shall drag them away, Surely he will cause their fold to be astounded over them.
text.KJV: 49:20 Therfore heare the counsell of the Lord, that he hath taken against Edom, & his purposes that hee hath purposed against the inhabitants of Teman: surely the least of the flocke shall draw them out: surely hee shall make their habitations desolate with them.
text.NABRE: Therefore, listen to the strategy the Lord devised for Edom; The plans he has drawn up against the inhabitants of Teman: They shall be dragged away, even the smallest of the flock; their pasture shall be aghast because of them.

text.NRSV: At the sound of their fall the earth shall quake; the sound of their cry shall be heard at the Red Sea.
text.Rotherham: At the noise of their fall, hath trembled the earth, At the outcry, in the Red Sea, was heard its noise.
text.KJV: 49:21 The earth is moued at the noise of their fall: at the crie, the noise thereof was heard in the red Sea.
text.NABRE: With the din of their collapse the earth quakes, to the Red Sea the outcry is heard!

text.NRSV: Look, one shall mount up and swoop down like an eagle, and spread his wings against Bozrah; and the heart of the warriors of Edom on that day shall be like the heart of a woman in labor.
text.Rotherham: Lo! as an eagle, he shall mount and dart, and spread his wings over Bozrah,—So shall the heart of the heroes of Edom, in that day, become as the heart of a woman in her pain.
text.KJV: 49:22 Behold, he shall come vp and flie as the eagle, and spread his wings ouer Bozrah: and at that day shall the heart of the mightie men of Edom, be as the heart of a woman in her pangs.
text.NABRE: Look! like an eagle he soars aloft, and spreads his wings over Bozrah; On that day the hearts of Edom’s warriors become like the heart of a woman in labor.

text.NRSV: Concerning Damascus. Hamath and Arpad are confounded, for they have heard bad news; they melt in fear, they are troubled like the sea that cannot be quiet.
text.Rotherham: Of Damascus. Turned pale have Hamath and Arpad, For, a calamitous report, have they heard—they tremble,—In the sea, is anxiety, it cannot, rest.
text.KJV: 49:23 ¶ Concerning Damascus, Hamath is confounded, & Arpad, for they haue heard euil tidings, they are faint hearted, there is sorrow on the sea, it can not be quiet.
text.NABRE: Against Damascus. Concerning Damascus. Hamath and Arpad are shamed, for they have heard bad news; Anxious, they surge like the sea which cannot calm down.

text.NRSV: Damascus has become feeble, she turned to flee, and panic seized her; anguish and sorrows have taken hold of her, as of a woman in labor.
text.Rotherham: Enfeebled is Damascus She hath turned to flee But terror, hath seized her,—Anguish and pangs, have seized her as a woman in childbirth.
text.KJV: 49:24 Damascus is waxed feeble, and turneth her selfe to flee, and feare hath seised on her: anguish and sorrowes haue taken her as a woman in trauell.
text.NABRE: Damascus loses heart, turns to flee; panic has seized it. Distress and pangs take hold, like the pain of a woman in labor.

text.NRSV: How the famous city is forsaken, the joyful town!
text.Rotherham: Alas! is she not forsaken—The city so praised! The citadel I rejoiced in!
text.KJV: 49:25 How is the citie of praise not left, the citie of my ioy?
text.NABRE: How can the glorious city be abandoned, the town of joy!

text.NRSV: Therefore her young men shall fall in her squares, and all her soldiers shall be destroyed on that day, says the Lord of hosts.
text.Rotherham: Therefore shall her young men fall in her broadways,—And, all her men of war, shall be silent in that day, Declareth Yahweh of hosts;
text.KJV: 49:26 Therefore her yong men shal fall in her streets, and all the men of warre shall bee cut off in that day, saith the Lord of hosts.
text.NABRE: But now its young men shall fall in its squares, all the warriors destroyed on that day— oracle of the Lord of hosts.

text.NRSV: And I will kindle a fire at the wall of Damascus, and it shall devour the strongholds of Ben-hadad.
text.Rotherham: Then will I kindle a fire in the walls of Damascus,—And it shall devour the palaces of Ben-hadad.
text.KJV: 49:27 And I will kindle a fire in the wall of Damascus, and it shal consume the palaces of Ben-hadad.
text.NABRE: I will set fire to the wall of Damascus; it shall devour the palaces of Ben-hadad.

text.NRSV: Concerning Kedar and the kingdoms of Hazor that King Nebuchadrezzar of Babylon defeated. Thus says the Lord: Rise up, advance against Kedar! Destroy the people of the east!
text.Rotherham: Of Kedar. And of the kingdoms of Hazor Which Nebuchadrezzar king of Babylon smote—Thus, saith Yahweh,—Arise ye go up against Kedar, So shall they spoil the sons of the East:
text.KJV: 49:28 ¶ Concerning Kedar, and concerning the kingdoms of Hazor, which Nebuchad-rezzar king of Babylon shall smite, Thus saith the Lord: Arise ye, goe vp to Kedar, and spoile the men of the East.
text.NABRE: Against Arabia. About Kedar and the kingdoms of Hazor, which Nebuchadnezzar, king of Babylon, defeated. Thus says the Lord: Rise up, attack Kedar, destroy the people from the east.

text.NRSV: Take their tents and their flocks, their curtains and all their goods; carry off their camels for yourselves, and a cry shall go up: “Terror is all around!”
text.Rotherham: Their tents and their flocks, shall they take, And their curtains and all their baggage and their camels, shall they carry off for themselves,—And shall cry out unto them, Terror round about!
text.KJV: 49:29 Their tents and their flocks shall they take away: they shal take to themselues their curtaines and all their vessels, and their camels, and they shal crie vnto them, Feare is on euery side.
text.NABRE: Their tents and flocks shall be taken away, their tent curtains and all their goods; Their camels they carry off, they shout over them, “Terror on every side!”

text.NRSV: Flee, wander far away, hide in deep places, O inhabitants of Hazor! says the Lord. For King Nebuchadrezzar of Babylon has made a plan against you and formed a purpose against you.
text.Rotherham: Flee remove far away, go deep to dwell, Ye inhabitants of Hazor, Urgeth Yahweh; For Nebuchadrezzar king of Babylon—hath counseled against you, a counsel, And devised against you a device.
text.KJV: 49:30 ¶ Flee, get you farre off, dwell deepe, O ye inhabitants of Hazor, saith the Lord: for Nebuchad-rezzar king of Babylon hath taken counsel against you, and hath conceiued a purpose against you.
text.NABRE: Flee! wander about, hide deep for lodging, inhabitants of Hazor—oracle of the Lord; For Nebuchadnezzar, king of Babylon, has devised a strategy against you, drawn up a plan against you,

text.NRSV: Rise up, advance against a nation at ease, that lives securely, says the Lord, that has no gates or bars, that lives alone.
text.Rotherham: Arise ye, go up against a nation at ease—dwelling securely, Commandeth Yahweh,—Having neither doors nor bars, Alone, do they dwell:
text.KJV: 49:31 Arise, get you vp vnto the wealthy nation that dwelleth without care, saith the Lord, which haue neither gates nor barres, which dwell alone.
text.NABRE: Get up! set out against a tranquil nation, living in security—oracle of the Lord — Without gates or bars, dwelling alone.

text.NRSV: Their camels shall become booty, their herds of cattle a spoil. I will scatter to every wind those who have shaven temples, and I will bring calamity upon them from every side, says the Lord.
text.Rotherham: So shall their camels, become, a prey, And the throng of their cattle, a spoil, Then will I scatter them to every wind even the clipt-beards,—And from all sides of him, will I bring in their doom Declareth Yahweh.
text.KJV: 49:32 And their camels shall be a bootie, and the multitude of their cattell a spoile, and I will scatter into all winds them that are in the vtmost corners, and I will bring their calamitie from all sides thereof, saith the Lord.
text.NABRE: Their camels shall become spoils, their hordes of cattle, plunder; I will scatter to the winds those who shave their temples; from every side I will bring their ruin— oracle of the Lord.

text.NRSV: Hazor shall become a lair of jackals, an everlasting waste; no one shall live there, nor shall anyone settle in it.
text.Rotherham: So shall Hazor become A habitation of jackals An astonishment unto times age-abiding: There shall not dwell there—a man, Nor sojourn therein—a son of the earth-born.
text.KJV: 49:33 And Hazor shall be a dwelling for dragons, and a desolation for euer; there shall no man abide there, nor any sonne of man dwell in it.
text.NABRE: Hazor shall become a haunt for jackals, a wasteland forever, Where no one lives, no mortal stays.

text.NRSV: The word of the Lord that came to the prophet Jeremiah concerning Elam, at the beginning of the reign of King Zedekiah of Judah.
text.Rotherham: So much of the word of Yahweh as came unto Jeremiah the prophet, Against Elam,—in the beginning of the reign of Zedekiah king of Judah saying:—
text.KJV: 49:34 ¶ The word of the Lord that came to Ieremiah the Prophet against Elam in the beginning of the reigne of Zedekiah king of Iudah, saying,
text.NABRE: Against Elam. The word of the Lord that came to Jeremiah the prophet concerning Elam at the beginning of the reign of Zedekiah, king of Judah:

text.NRSV: Thus says the Lord of hosts: I am going to break the bow of Elam, the mainstay of their might;
text.Rotherham: Thus, saith Yahweh of hosts, Behold me! breaking the bow of Elam,—The beginning of their might;
text.KJV: 49:35 Thus saith the Lord of hosts, Behold, I will breake the bow of Elam, the chiefe of their might.
text.NABRE: Thus says the Lord of hosts: Look! I will break the bow of Elam, the mainstay of their might.

text.NRSV: and I will bring upon Elam the four winds from the four quarters of heaven; and I will scatter them to all these winds, and there shall be no nation to which the exiles from Elam shall not come.
text.Rotherham: Then will I bring in against Elam four winds from the four quarters of the heavens, And will scatter them to all these winds,—And there shall be no nation whither the outcasts of Elam, shall not come;
text.KJV: 49:36 And vpon Elam will I bring the foure windes from the foure quarters of heauen, and will scatter them towards all those windes, and there shall be no nation, whither the outcasts of Elam shall not come.
text.NABRE: I will bring upon Elam the four winds from the four ends of the heavens: I will scatter them to all these winds, until there is no nation to which the outcasts of Elam have not gone.

text.NRSV: I will terrify Elam before their enemies, and before those who seek their life; I will bring disaster upon them, my fierce anger, says the Lord. I will send the sword after them, until I have consumed them;
text.Rotherham: And I will cause Elam to be dismayed Before their enemies Even before them who are seeking their life, So will I bring upon them calamity, Even the glow of mine anger Declareth Yahweh,—And will send after them the sword, until I have made an end of them;
text.KJV: 49:37 For I will cause Elam to bee dismayed before their enemies, and before them that seeke their life: and I will bring euill vpon them, euen my fierce anger, saith the Lord, and I will send the sword after them, till I haue consumed them.
text.NABRE: I will terrify Elam before their foes, those seeking their life; I will bring evil upon them, my burning wrath—oracle of the Lord. I will send sword after them until I have finished them off;

text.NRSV: and I will set my throne in Elam, and destroy their king and officials, says the Lord.
text.Rotherham: And I will set my throne in Elam,—And will destroy, from thence king and princes, Declareth Yahweh;
text.KJV: 49:38 And I will set my throne in Elam, and will destroy from thence the king and the princes, saith the Lord.
text.NABRE: I will set up my throne in Elam and destroy from there king and princes— oracle of the Lord.

text.NRSV: But in the latter days I will restore the fortunes of Elam, says the Lord.
text.Rotherham: But it shall come to pass In the afterpart of the days, That I will bring back the captivity of Elam, Declareth Yahweh.
text.KJV: 49:39 ¶ But it shall come to passe in the later daies, that I wil bring againe the captiuitie of Elam, saith the Lord.
text.NABRE: But at the end of days I will restore the fortunes of Elam—oracle of the Lord.

text.NRSV: The word that the Lord spoke concerning Babylon, concerning the land of the Chaldeans, by the prophet Jeremiah:
text.Rotherham: The word which Yahweh spake Against Babylon Against the land of the Chaldeans, through Jeremiah the prophet:
text.KJV: 50:1 The word that the Lord spake against Babylon, and against the land of the Caldeans by Ieremiah the Prophet.
text.NABRE: Chapter 50 The First Oracle Against Babylon. The word the Lord spoke against Babylon, against the land of the Chaldeans, through Jeremiah the prophet:

text.NRSV: Declare among the nations and proclaim, set up a banner and proclaim; do not conceal it, say: Babylon is taken, Bel is put to shame, Merodach is dismayed. Her images are put to shame, her idols are dismayed.
text.Rotherham: Tell ye among the nations And let it be heard And lift ye up a standard, Let it be heard do not conceal: Say ye—Captured is Babylon, Confounded is Bel, Broken in pieces is Merodach, Confounded are her images, Broken down her manufactured gods;
text.KJV: 50:2 Declare yee among the nations, and publish, and set vp a standart, publish and conceale not: say, Babylon is taken, Bel is confounded, Merodach is broken in pieces, her idols are confounded, her Images are broken in pieces.
text.NABRE: Proclaim this among the nations, announce it! Announce it, do not hide it, but say: Babylon is captured, Bel put to shame, Marduk terrified; its images are put to shame, its idols shattered.

text.NRSV: For out of the north a nation has come up against her; it shall make her land a desolation, and no one shall live in it; both human beings and animals shall flee away.
text.Rotherham: For there hath come up against her—a nation out of the North The same, shall make her land an astonishment, And there shall be none to dwell therein,—Both man and beast, have removed—have gone.
text.KJV: 50:3 For out of the North there commeth vp a nation against her, which shall make her land desolate, and none shall dwell therein: they shall remoue, they shall depart both man and beast.
text.NABRE: A nation from the north advances against it, making the land desolate So that no one can live there; human beings and animals have fled.

text.NRSV: In those days and at that time, says the Lord, the people of Israel shall come, they and the people of Judah together; they shall come weeping as they seek the Lord their God.
text.Rotherham: In those days, and at that time, Declareth Yahweh, Shall the sons of Israel come in, They and the sons of Judah together: Weeping as they travel, so shall they journey on, And Yahweh their God, shall they seek;
text.KJV: 50:4 ¶ In those daies, and in that time, saith the Lord, the children of Israel shall come, they, and the children of Iudah together, going and weeping: they shall goe, and seeke the Lord their God.
text.NABRE: In those days and at that time—oracle of the Lord — Israelite and Judahite shall come together, Weeping as they come, to seek the Lord, their God;

text.NRSV: They shall ask the way to Zion, with faces turned toward it, and they shall come and join themselves to the Lord by an everlasting covenant that will never be forgotten.
text.Rotherham: To Zion, shall they ask the way, Hitherward, their faces, Come and let us join ourselves unto Yahweh, In a covenant age-abiding, which shall not be forgotten.
text.KJV: 50:5 They shall aske the way to Zion with their faces thitherward, saying, Come, and let vs ioyne our selues to the Lord, in a perpetuall Couenant that shall not be forgotten.
text.NABRE: They shall ask for Zion, seeking out the way. “Come, let us join ourselves to the Lord in an everlasting covenant, never to be forgotten.”

text.NRSV: My people have been lost sheep; their shepherds have led them astray, turning them away on the mountains; from mountain to hill they have gone, they have forgotten their fold.
text.Rotherham: Wandering sheep, have my people, been, Their own shepherds led them astray, On the mountains, they seduced them, From mountain to hill, have they gone, They have forgotten their couching-place.
text.KJV: 50:6 My people hath bene lost sheepe: their shepheards haue caused them to goe astray, they haue turned them away on the mountaines: they haue gone from mountaine to hill, they haue forgotten their resting place.
text.NABRE: Lost sheep were my people, their shepherds misled them, leading them astray on the mountains; From mountain to hill they wandered, forgetting their fold.

text.NRSV: All who found them have devoured them, and their enemies have said, “We are not guilty, because they have sinned against the Lord, the true pasture, the Lord, the hope of their ancestors.”
text.Rotherham: All who found them, devoured them, And, their adversaries, said—We shall not be, guilty,—Because they have sinned, Against, Yahweh the pasturage of righteousness, Yea the hope of their fathers—Yahweh.
text.KJV: 50:7 All that found them haue deuoured them, and their aduersaries said, We offend not, because they haue sinned against the Lord, the habitation of iustice, euen the Lord, the hope of their fathers.
text.NABRE: Whoever happened upon them devoured them; their enemies said, “We are not guilty, Because they sinned against the Lord, the abode of justice, the hope of their ancestors.”

text.NRSV: Flee from Babylon, and go out of the land of the Chaldeans, and be like male goats leading the flock.
text.Rotherham: Remove ye out of the midst of Babylon, And out of the land of the Chaldeans, come ye forth,—And become ye like he-goats before the flock;
text.KJV: 50:8 Remoue out of the midst of Babylon, and goe foorth out of the land of the Caldeans, and be as the hee goats before the flocks.
text.NABRE: Flee from the midst of Babylon, leave the land of the Chaldeans, be like rams at the head of the flock.

text.NRSV: For I am going to stir up and bring against Babylon a company of great nations from the land of the north; and they shall array themselves against her; from there she shall be taken. Their arrows are like the arrows of a skilled warrior who does not return empty-handed.
text.Rotherham: For lo! I am rousing and bringing up against Babylon a gathered host of great nations, out of the land of the North, And they shall array themselves against her, From thence, shall she be captured,—His arrows, are as of a hero making childless, None shall return empty.
text.KJV: 50:9 ¶ For loe, I will raise and cause to come vp against Babylon, an assembly of great nations from the North countrey, and they shall set themselues in aray against her, from thence shee shalbe taken: their arrowes shalbe as of a mightie expert man: none shall returne in vaine.
text.NABRE: See, I am stirring up against Babylon a band of great nations from the land of the north; They are arrayed against her, from there she shall be taken. Their arrows are like the arrows of a skilled warrior who never returns empty-handed.

text.NRSV: Chaldea shall be plundered; all who plunder her shall be sated, says the Lord.
text.Rotherham: So shall the Chaldeans become a spoil,—All her spoilers, shall be satisfied, Declareth Yahweh;
text.KJV: 50:10 And Caldea shall bee a spoile: all that spoile her shall be satisfied, saith the Lord.
text.NABRE: Chaldea shall become plunder; all its plunderers shall be enriched— oracle of the Lord.

text.NRSV: Though you rejoice, though you exult, O plunderers of my heritage, though you frisk like a heifer on the grass, and neigh like stallions,
text.Rotherham: Because they used to be glad Because they used to be uproarious When plundering mine inheritance,—Because they used to caper about as a heifer at grass, And bellow like bulls.
text.KJV: 50:11 Because ye were glad, because yee reioyced, O ye destroyers of mine heritage, because ye are growen fat, as the heifer at grasse, and bellow as bulles:
text.NABRE: Yes, rejoice and exult, you that plunder my heritage; Frisk like calves on the grass, neigh like stallions!

text.NRSV: your mother shall be utterly shamed, and she who bore you shall be disgraced. Lo, she shall be the last of the nations, a wilderness, dry land, and a desert.
text.Rotherham: Your mother, hath turned very pale, She that bare you, hath turned red,—Lo! the last of nations, is—A desert, A parched land, and A waste plain,
text.KJV: 50:12 Your mother shalbe sore confounded, she that bare you shalbe ashamed: beholde, the hindermost of the nations shalbe a wildernes, a dry land, & a desert.
text.NABRE: Your mother will indeed be put to shame, she that bore you shall be abashed; See, the last of the nations, a wilderness, a dry wasteland.

text.NRSV: Because of the wrath of the Lord she shall not be inhabited, but shall be an utter desolation; everyone who passes by Babylon shall be appalled and hiss because of all her wounds.
text.Rotherham: Because of the vexation of Yahweh, she shall not be inhabited, But Shall become a complete desolation,—Every one passing by Babylon, shall be astonished and hiss, over all her plagues.
text.KJV: 50:13 Because of the wrath of the Lord, it shall not be inhabited, but it shalbe wholly desolate: euery one that goeth by Babylon shall be astonished, and hisse at all her plagues.
text.NABRE: Because of the Lord ’s wrath it shall be uninhabited, become an utter wasteland; Everyone who passes by Babylon will be appalled and hiss at all its wounds.

text.NRSV: Array yourselves against Babylon all around, all you who bend the bow; shoot at her, spare no arrows, for she has sinned against the Lord.
text.Rotherham: Set yourselves in array against Babylon round about All ye who tread the bow, Shoot at her, do not spare so much as an arrow,—For against Yahweh, hath she sinned.
text.KJV: 50:14 Put your selues in aray against Babylon round about: all ye that bend the bow, shoot at her; spare no arrows: for she hath sinned against the Lord,
text.NABRE: Take your posts encircling Babylon, you who bend the bow; Shoot at it, do not spare your arrows,

text.NRSV: Raise a shout against her from all sides, she has surrendered; her bulwarks have fallen, her walls are thrown down. For this is the vengeance of the Lord: take vengeance on her, do to her as she has done.
text.Rotherham: Raise a shout against her round about She hath stretched forth her hand, Fallen are her buttresses, Torn down are her walls,—Because, the avenging of Yahweh it is, Take ye vengeance upon her, As she hath done, do ye, unto her.
text.KJV: 50:15 Shout against her round about: shee hath giuen her hand: her foundations are fallen, her walls are throwen downe: for it is the vengeance of the Lord: take vengeance vpon her; as she hath done, doe vnto her.
text.NABRE: raise the war cry against it on every side. It surrenders, its bastions fall, its walls are torn down: This is retribution from the Lord! Take retribution on her, as she has done, do to her; for she sinned against the Lord.

text.NRSV: Cut off from Babylon the sower, and the one who wields the sickle in time of harvest; because of the destroying sword, all of them shall face their own people, and all of them shall flee to their own land.
text.Rotherham: Cut ye off the sower from Babylon, And him that graspeth the sickle in the time of harvest, From the face of the sword of the oppressor, Each, to his own people will they turn, and Each, to his own land will they flee.
text.KJV: 50:16 Cut off the sower from Babylon, and him that handleth the sickle in the time of haruest: for feare of the oppressing sword, they shall turne euery one to his people, and they shall flee euery one to his owne lande.
text.NABRE: Cut off the sower from Babylon and those who wield sickles at harvest time! Before the destroying sword, all of them turn back to their own people, all flee to their own land.

text.NRSV: Israel is a hunted sheep driven away by lions. First the king of Assyria devoured it, and now at the last King Nebuchadrezzar of Babylon has gnawed its bones.
text.Rotherham: A sheep all alone, is Israel Lions, have driven him away,—At the first, the king of Assyria devoured him, And here, at the last, Nebuchadrezzar king of Babylon hath broken his bones!
text.KJV: 50:17 ¶ Israel is a scattered sheepe, the lyons haue driuen him away: first the king of Assyria hath deuoured him, and last this Nebuchad-rezzar king of Babylon hath broken his bones.
text.NABRE: Israel was a stray sheep that lions pursued; The king of Assyria once devoured him; now Nebuchadnezzar of Babylon gnaws his bones.

text.NRSV: Therefore, thus says the Lord of hosts, the God of Israel: I am going to punish the king of Babylon and his land, as I punished the king of Assyria.
text.Rotherham: Therefore, Thus, saith Yahweh of hosts, God of Israel, Behold me! bringing punishment against the king of Babylon, and against his land,—Just as I brought punishment against the king of Assyria.
text.KJV: 50:18 Therefore thus saith the Lord of hostes the God of Israel; Behold, I will punish the king of Babylon and his land, as I haue punished the king of Assyria.
text.NABRE: Therefore, thus says the Lord of hosts, the God of Israel: I will punish the king of Babylon and his land, as I once punished the king of Assyria;

text.NRSV: I will restore Israel to its pasture, and it shall feed on Carmel and in Bashan, and its desire shall be satisfied on the hills of Ephraim and in Gilead.
text.Rotherham: So will I bring back Israel unto his own pasture, And he shall feed upon Carmel and Bashan; And in the hill country of Ephraim and Gilead, shall, his soul, be satisfied.
text.KJV: 50:19 And I will bring Israel againe to his habitation, and he shal feed on Carmel and Bashan, and his soule shall be satisfied vpon mount Ephraim and Gilead.
text.NABRE: But I will bring Israel back to its pasture, to feed on Carmel and Bashan, And on Mount Ephraim and Gilead, until they have their fill.

text.NRSV: In those days and at that time, says the Lord, the iniquity of Israel shall be sought, and there shall be none; and the sins of Judah, and none shall be found; for I will pardon the remnant that I have spared.
text.Rotherham: In those days and in that time, Declareth Yahweh, The iniquity of Israel, shall be sought, and there shall be none, And the sins of Judah, and they shall not be found; For I will grant pardon to them whom I suffer to remain.
text.KJV: 50:20 In those dayes, and in that time, sayth the Lord, the iniquitie of Israel shall be sought for, and there shall be none; and the sinnes of Iudah, and they shall not be found: for I will pardon them whom I reserue.
text.NABRE: In those days, at that time—oracle of the Lord: The guilt of Israel may be sought, but it no longer exists, the sin of Judah, but it can no longer be found; for I will forgive the remnant I preserve.

text.NRSV: Go up to the land of Merathaim; go up against it, and against the inhabitants of Pekod and utterly destroy the last of them, says the Lord; do all that I have commanded you.
text.Rotherham: Against the land of Merathaim, go thou up against her, And against the inhabitants of Pekod,—Lay waste and devote to destruction after them Declareth Yahweh, And do according to all which I have commanded thee.
text.KJV: 50:21 ¶ Goe vp against the land of Merathaim, euen against it, and against the inhabitants of Pekod: waste and vtterly destroy after them, sayeth the Lord, and doe according to all that I haue commanded thee.
text.NABRE: Attack the land of Merathaim, and those who live in Pekod; Slaughter and put them under the ban—oracle of the Lord — do all I have commanded you.

text.NRSV: The noise of battle is in the land, and great destruction!
text.Rotherham: The sound of battle is in the earth,—And a great destruction.
text.KJV: 50:22 A sound of battell is in the land, and of great destruction.
text.NABRE: Battle alarm in the land, great destruction!

text.NRSV: How the hammer of the whole earth is cut down and broken! How Babylon has become a horror among the nations!
text.Rotherham: How is cut and broken the hammer of all the earth! How hath Babylon become an astonishment among the nations!
text.KJV: 50:23 How is the hammer of the whole earth cut asunder and broken? how is Babylon become a desolation among the nations?
text.NABRE: How the hammer of the whole earth has been cut off and broken! What an object of horror Babylon has become among the nations!

text.NRSV: I set a snare for you and you were caught, O Babylon, but you did not know it; you were found and seized, because you challenged the Lord.
text.Rotherham: I laid a snare for thee yea and thou wast captured O Babylon, when, thou wast not aware,—Thou wast found out yea and taken, For with Yahweh, hadst thou contended.
text.KJV: 50:24 I haue laide a snare for thee, and thou art also taken, O Babylon, and thou wast not aware: thou art found and also caught, because thou hast striuen against the Lord.
text.NABRE: You ensnared yourself and were caught, Babylon, before you knew it! You were discovered and seized, because you challenged the Lord.

text.NRSV: The Lord has opened his armory, and brought out the weapons of his wrath, for the Lord God of hosts has a task to do in the land of the Chaldeans.
text.Rotherham: Yahweh opened his armoury, and brought out his weapons of indignation,—For, a work, it is pertaining to My Lord, Yahweh of hosts, in the land of the Chaldeans,
text.KJV: 50:25 The Lord hath opened his armorie, and hath brought foorth the weapons of his indignation: for this is the worke of the Lord God of hosts, in the land of the Caldeans.
text.NABRE: The Lord opens his armory, brings out the weapons of his wrath; The Lord God of hosts has work to do in the land of the Chaldeans.

text.NRSV: Come against her from every quarter; open her granaries; pile her up like heaps of grain, and utterly destroy her; let nothing be left of her.
text.Rotherham: Come ye against her from farthest parts, Throw open her granaries, Cast her up as heaps, and devote her to destruction,—Do not let her have a remnant.
text.KJV: 50:26 Come against her from the vtmost border, open her store-houses: cast her vp as heapes, and destroy her vtterly: let nothing of her be left.
text.NABRE: Come upon them from every side, open their granaries, Pile them up in heaps and put them under the ban; do not leave a remnant.

text.NRSV: Kill all her bulls, let them go down to the slaughter. Alas for them, for their day has come, the time of their punishment!
text.Rotherham: Cut up all her bullocks, Let them go down to the slaughter,—Alas for them! For their day, hath come, Their time for punishment.
text.KJV: 50:27 Slay all her bullocks: let them goe downe to the slaughter: woe vnto them, for their day is come, the time of their visitation.
text.NABRE: Slay all the oxen, take them down to slaughter; Woe to them! their day has come, the time of their punishment.

text.NRSV: Listen! Fugitives and refugees from the land of Babylon are coming to Zion to declare the vengeance of the Lord our God, vengeance for his temple.
text.Rotherham: The voice Of them who are in flight and Of such as are escaping, out of the land of Babylon,—To tell in Zion The avenging of Yahweh our God, The avenging of his temple.
text.KJV: 50:28 The voice of them that flee & escape out of the land of Babylon to declare in Zion the vengeance of the Lord our God, the vengeance of his Temple.
text.NABRE: Listen! the fugitives, the refugees from the land of Babylon: They announce in Zion the retribution of the Lord, our God.

text.NRSV: Summon archers against Babylon, all who bend the bow. Encamp all around her; let no one escape. Repay her according to her deeds; just as she has done, do to her—for she has arrogantly defied the Lord, the Holy One of Israel.
text.Rotherham: Publish against Babylon ye chiefs of all who tread the bow—Encamp against her round about Let there be none to escape, Recompense to her according to her work, According to all which she did, do ye to her,—For Against Yahweh, hath she acted presumptuously, Against the Holy One of Israel.
text.KJV: 50:29 Call together the archers against Babylon: all yee that bend the bow, campe against it round about; let none thereof escape: recompense her according to her worke; according to all that shee hath done vnto her: for shee hath bene proud against the Lord, against the Holy one of Israel.
text.NABRE: Call archers out against Babylon, all who bend the bow; Encamp around them; let no one escape. Repay them for their deeds; what they have done, do to them, For they insulted the Lord, the Holy One of Israel.

text.NRSV: Therefore her young men shall fall in her squares, and all her soldiers shall be destroyed on that day, says the Lord.
text.Rotherham: Therefore, shall her young men fall in her broadways,—And, all her men of war, be silenced in that day, Declareth Yahweh.
text.KJV: 50:30 Therefore shall her yong men fall in the streets, & all her men of war shall be cut off in that day, saith the Lord.
text.NABRE: Therefore their young men shall fall in the squares, all their warriors shall be stilled on that day— oracle of the Lord.

text.NRSV: I am against you, O arrogant one, says the Lord God of hosts; for your day has come, the time when I will punish you.
text.Rotherham: Behold me! against thee most insolent one, Declareth My Lord Yahweh of hosts; For thy day, hath come, Thy time for punishment;
text.KJV: 50:31 Behold, I am against thee, O thou most proud, sayth the Lord God of hostes: for thy day is come, the time that I will visit thee.
text.NABRE: I am against you, O Insolence— oracle of the Lord God of hosts; For your day has come, the time for me to punish you.

text.NRSV: The arrogant one shall stumble and fall, with no one to raise him up, and I will kindle a fire in his cities, and it will devour everything around him.
text.Rotherham: So shall the most insolent one stumble and fall, And he shall have none to lift him up,—And I will kindle a fire in his cities which shall devour all who are round about him.
text.KJV: 50:32 And the most proude shall stumble and fall, and none shal raise him vp: and I will kindle a fire in his cities, and it shall deuoure all round about him.
text.NABRE: Insolence stumbles and falls; there is no one to raise him up. I will kindle a fire in his cities to devour everything around him.

text.NRSV: Thus says the Lord of hosts: The people of Israel are oppressed, and the people of Judah too; all who took them captive shut them up, they refuse to let them go.
text.Rotherham: Thus, saith Yahweh of hosts—Oppressed were the sons of Israel and the sons of Judah, together,—And, all who took them captive, Held them fast Refused to let them go:—
text.KJV: 50:33 ¶ Thus saith the Lord of hosts; The children of Israel and the children of Iudah were oppressed together, and all that tooke them captiues, held them fast, they refused to let them goe.
text.NABRE: Thus says the Lord of hosts: Oppressed are the people of Israel, together with the people of Judah; All their captors hold them fast and refuse to let them go.

text.NRSV: Their Redeemer is strong; the Lord of hosts is his name. He will surely plead their cause, that he may give rest to the earth, but unrest to the inhabitants of Babylon.
text.Rotherham: Their Redeemer, can hold fast, Yahweh of hosts, is his name, He will, thoroughly plead, their plea,—That he may quiet the earth, And disquiet the inhabitants of Babylon.
text.KJV: 50:34 Their Redeemer is strong, the Lord of hosts is his Name, he shall throughly pleade their cause, that hee may giue rest to the land, and disquiet the inhabitants of Babylon.
text.NABRE: Strong is their Redeemer, whose name is Lord of hosts, The sure defender of their cause, who gives rest to their land, but unrest to those who live in Babylon.

text.NRSV: A sword against the Chaldeans, says the Lord, and against the inhabitants of Babylon, and against her officials and her sages!
text.Rotherham: A sword, is over the Chaldeans, Declareth Yahweh,—And against the inhabitants of Babylon, And against her princes, And against her wise men:
text.KJV: 50:35 ¶ A sword is vpon the Caldeans, saith the Lord, and vpon the inhabitants of Babylon, and vpon her princes, and vpon her wise men.
text.NABRE: A sword upon the Chaldeans—oracle of the Lord — upon the inhabitants of Babylon, her princes and sages!

text.NRSV: A sword against the diviners, so that they may become fools! A sword against her warriors, so that they may be destroyed!
text.Rotherham: A sword, is against the praters, And they shall be shewn to be foolish, A sword, is against her heroes And they shall be dismayed:
text.KJV: 50:36 A sword is vpon the lyers, and they shall dote: a sword is vpon her mighty men, and they shalbe dismayed.
text.NABRE: A sword upon the soothsayers, and they become fools! A sword upon the warriors, and they tremble;

text.NRSV: A sword against her horses and against her chariots, and against all the mixed peoples in her midst, so that they may become women! A sword against her treasures, so that they may be plundered!
text.Rotherham: A sword, is against his horses and against his chariots and, against all the rabble that are in her midst And they shall become women,—A sword, is against her treasures And they shall be made a prey:
text.KJV: 50:37 A sword is vpon their horses, and vpon their charets, and vpon all the mingled people that are in the middest of her, and they shall become as women: a sword is vpon her treasures, and they shall be robbed.
text.NABRE: A sword upon their motley throng, and they become women! A sword upon their treasures, and they are plundered;

text.NRSV: A drought against her waters, so that they may be dried up! For it is a land of images, and they go mad over idols.
text.Rotherham: A drought, is against her waters, And they shall be dried up,—For a land of images, it is, And with their shocking things, they act as men who are mad:
text.KJV: 50:38 A drought is vpon her waters, and they shalbe dried vp: for it is the land of grauen images, and they are madde vpon their idoles.
text.NABRE: A drought upon the waters, and they dry up! For it is a land of idols, soon made frantic by phantoms.

text.NRSV: Therefore wild animals shall live with hyenas in Babylon, and ostriches shall inhabit her; she shall never again be peopled, or inhabited for all generations.
text.Rotherham: Therefore shall the criers dwell with, the howlers, Yea ostriches, shall dwell therein,—So shall it be dwelt in no more for ever, Neither shall it be inhabited unto generation after generation.
text.KJV: 50:39 Therefore the wilde beasts of the desert with the wilde beastes of the Ilands shall dwel there, and the owles shall dwell therein: & it shalbe no more inhabited for euer: neither shall it bee dwelt in frō generation to generation.
text.NABRE: Hence, wildcats shall dwell there with hyenas, and ostriches occupy it; Never again shall it be inhabited or settled, from age to age.

text.NRSV: As when God overthrew Sodom and Gomorrah and their neighbors, says the Lord, so no one shall live there, nor shall anyone settle in her.
text.Rotherham: Like the divine overthrow of Sodom and Gomorrah and her neighbours, Declareth Yahweh,—There shall not dwell there—a man, Nor sojourn therein—a son of the earth-born.
text.KJV: 50:40 As God ouerthrew Sodom and Gomorrah, and the neighbour cities thereof, sayth the Lord: so shall no man abide there, neither shal any sonne of man dwell therein.
text.NABRE: As happened when God overturned Sodom and Gomorrah and their neighbors—oracle of the Lord — No one shall dwell there, no mortal shall settle there.

text.NRSV: Look, a people is coming from the north; a mighty nation and many kings are stirring from the farthest parts of the earth.
text.Rotherham: Lo! a people coming in from the North,—Yea, a great nation and many kings, shall be roused up out of the remote parts of the earth:
text.KJV: 50:41 Behold, a people shall come from the North, and a great nation, and many kings shall bee raised vp from the coasts of the earth.
text.NABRE: See, a people comes from the north, a great nation, and mighty kings rising from the ends of the earth.

text.NRSV: They wield bow and spear, they are cruel and have no mercy. The sound of them is like the roaring sea; they ride on horses, arrayed as a warrior for battle against you, O daughter Babylon!
text.Rotherham: Bow and javelin, shall they grasp, Cruel, are they and will not have compassion, Their voice, like the sea, will roar, And, on horses, will they ride,—Set in array, as one man for battle, Against thee, O daughter of Babylon!
text.KJV: 50:42 They shall holde the bow and the lance: they are cruell and will not shewe mercy: their voice shall roare like the sea, and they shall ride vpon horses, euery one put in aray like a man to the battell, against thee, O daughter of Babylon.
text.NABRE: Bow and javelin they wield, cruel and pitiless are they; They sound like the roaring sea, as they ride forth on horses, Each in place for battle against you, daughter Babylon.

text.NRSV: The king of Babylon heard news of them, and his hands fell helpless; anguish seized him, pain like that of a woman in labor.
text.Rotherham: The king of Babylon hath heard the report of them, And unnerved are his hands,—Anguish, hath seized him, Writhing pain, as a woman in child-birth.
text.KJV: 50:43 The king of Babylon hath heard the report of them, and his hands waxed feeble; anguish tooke hold of him, and pangs as of a woman in trauell.
text.NABRE: The king of Babylon hears news of them, and his hands hang helpless; Anguish takes hold of him, like the pangs of a woman giving birth.

text.NRSV: Like a lion coming up from the thickets of the Jordan against a perennial pasture, I will suddenly chase them away from her; and I will appoint over her whomever I choose. For who is like me? Who can summon me? Who is the shepherd who can stand before me?
text.Rotherham: Lo! as a lion, shall he come up from the majesty of the Jordan, Unto the pasture perennial, But I will wink—I will make them run away therefrom, Who then shall be the Chosen One whom against it, I may set in charge?
